高雄淨觀講堂

靜心・修心・覺心媽祖慈悲　帝爺正氣

天地能量，指引人生

在一片安靜之中，讓心慢慢落下來。
一呼一吸之間，身心安住，
方向，自然清明。
預約諮詢 了解更多
地址
高雄市三民區
建國三路83號6樓
電話
+886-7-2859118
電子郵件
[email protected]
營業時間
每週六・日
13:00 – 17:00
Facebook
淨觀講堂官方粉絲頁
我們的服務

為每一顆迷惘的心
點一盞明燈

靜心修行

透過靜坐與修心練習，學會觀照自己、放下執著，重新與內在連結，找回生命的安定感。

  • 靜坐引導課程
  • 呼吸與專注練習
  • 內觀修心工作坊

信仰祈福

媽祖慈悲守護，關聖帝爺正氣安住，透過信仰的力量，為生活帶來平安與祝福。

  • 點燈祈福・平安祈願
  • 法會祝禱・心靈淨化
  • 神明指引問事

人生諮詢

透過信仰與智慧的指引，幫助您在迷惘中看見方向，釐清人生課題、事業發展與心靈困惑。

  • 感情與家庭問題
  • 事業方向與抉擇
  • 身心靈整體諮詢
關於我們
淨靜之境
觀悟己心

淨觀講堂座落於高雄市三民區，是一處融合信仰、靜心與心靈諮詢的修行空間。我們以媽祖慈悲之心、關聖帝爺的浩然正氣為依歸，協助每一位前來的人，在人生的迷茫中找到方向。

不論您正面臨情感困境、事業抉擇，還是內心的迷惘與不安，我們都以開放、不評判的態度陪伴您——讓天地能量，成為您前行的力量。

深入了解
黃老師語錄

師語智慧

媽祖的慈悲，不是替你解決所有問題，而是讓你有勇氣，面對自己的問題。

— 黃老師

修行不是逃離生活，而是讓你在生活中，活得更清醒、更安定、更像自己。

— 黃老師

問神、問人，都不如先問自己。很多答案，你其實早就知道了。

— 黃老師

靜心 · 修心 · 覺心
媽祖慈悲，帝爺正氣
天地能量，指引人生

© 2026 高雄淨觀講堂　版權所有媽祖慈悲，守護每一顆心

高雄淨觀講堂

關於講堂

淨觀講堂是一處融合禪修、信仰與心靈的道場。有媽祖慈悲的守護，也有關帝爺正氣的加持，溫柔地陪伴每一位走進來的人。

與自己重新相遇的旅程

淨觀講堂座落於高雄市三民區，是一處融合信仰、靜心與心靈諮詢的修行空間。我們以媽祖慈悲之心、關聖帝爺的浩然正氣為依歸，協助每一位前來的人，在人生的迷茫中找到方向。

在繁忙、壓力與紛擾交織的現代生活中，我們為每一位需要的人，開放一處安心停靠的地方。在這裡，你可以放下外在的疲憊與角色，靜靜與自己相處，讓心慢慢沉澱，回到最真實的狀態。

不論您正面臨情感困境、事業抉擇，還是內心的迷惘與不安，我們都以開放、不評判的態度陪伴您——讓天地能量，成為您前行的力量。

一

媽祖信仰守護

以媽祖慈悲能量安定人心、化解不安，幫助人們在生活中找到平靜與力量。

二

帝君正氣加持

透過帝君精神的學習，幫助人培養堅定的內在力量，在人生道路上走得穩定清明。

三

修心內在覺察

透過靜坐、觀照與覺察，慢慢學會看見自己的情緒與想法，找到真正的平靜。

預約諮詢

媽祖慈悲，帝爺正氣

© 2026 高雄淨觀講堂媽祖慈悲，守護每一顆心

心靈智慧

信仰・修行・智慧

從媽祖信仰到帝君精神，從靜心修行到人生智慧，每一篇都是一盞指引的燈。

一・媽祖信仰

媽祖的慈悲守護

信仰是心靈最溫柔的依靠

媽祖是華人信仰中最具慈悲與守護力量的神明之一。自古以來，無數人向媽祖祈求平安、順利與指引。在信仰中，媽祖象徵著母親般的關懷與守護。

在淨觀講堂中，我們相信媽祖的慈悲能量能夠安定人心、化解不安，幫助人們在生活中找到平靜與力量。當心中充滿信任與善念時，媽祖的守護也會自然地陪伴在我們身邊。

二・帝君精神

關聖帝君的正氣與智慧

正直的心，是最強大的力量

關聖帝君是忠義與正氣的象徵。在傳統文化中，他代表著誠信、勇氣與正直。帝君精神提醒我們，在人生道路上應該保持正念、守護道義，以正直的心面對每一個選擇。

三・修心覺察

回到內在的平靜

修行，是重新認識自己的旅程

現代生活節奏快速，許多人在忙碌與壓力之中逐漸失去內心的平衡。修心的過程，其實是一種重新認識自己的旅程。透過靜坐、觀照與覺察，我們慢慢學會看見自己的情緒與想法。修心不是逃避生活，而是在生活之中找到真正的平靜。

四・人生智慧

在人生之中找到方向

答案，一直都在你心中

人生的道路並不總是清晰。有時候，我們會遇到困惑與迷惘。當我們願意靜下心來，傾聽內在的聲音，很多問題的答案其實就在自己的心中。真正的人生智慧，不是逃避困難，而是在經歷之中成長，並找到屬於自己的道路。

© 2026 高雄淨觀講堂媽祖慈悲，守護每一顆心

黃老師語錄

師語智慧

每一句話，都是在某個當下，從心裡流出來的。靜靜讀著，或許也能找到屬於你的那句話。

不再問什麼時候，此刻就是答案

與其問「什麼時候」，不如讓心歇一歇。靜靜地聽著，靜靜地看著，靜靜地做著該做的事，靜靜地等著該來的緣，也靜靜地守著那份不動的心。因為「時候」，不在遠方，就在一個呼吸之間。當心靜了，萬物皆靜；當心明了，萬事皆明。

媽祖在人間　志工的身影即是慈悲的化身

當你看見一個人，默默地付出，不求回報，那就是媽祖在人間的樣子。志工的身影，就是慈悲最具體的化身。不需要神蹟，不需要儀式，只需要一顆願意給予的心。我們每一個人，都可以是媽祖在人間的一雙手。

錢非萬能．媽祖濟世不收錢

因為慈悲，本來就沒有價格。真正的幫助，是讓人站起來，而不是讓人依賴。媽祖的心，是希望每個人都能找到自己的力量，而不是永遠需要靠著神明才能走路。緣金隨喜，是一種心意；不是門票，也不是條件。

承蒙關照　道法自然

人生有很多事，不是努力就能控制的。有時候，放手，才是最大的智慧。讓事情自然發展，讓緣分自然流動，讓答案自然浮現。道法自然，不是消極，而是一種更深的信任——信任自己，信任天地，信任這一切的安排都有它的道理。

我會好好的

不管現在多難，不管此刻多痛，記得告訴自己：我會好好的。這不是自我欺騙，這是一種選擇。選擇相信自己有能力走過去，選擇相信時間會帶來改變。媽祖慈悲守護著你，你也要好好守護自己。

活動與資訊・線上預約

讓我們陪您
找到方向

無論您面對的是情感、事業、家庭或心靈困境，歡迎填寫以下表單，我們將盡快與您聯繫安排時間。

歡迎前來淨觀講堂

媽祖慈悲濟世不收費，緣金隨喜，心意即可。我們只希望每個人都能找到屬於自己的方向。

  • 地址
    高雄市三民區建國三路83號6樓（御宿商旅對面）
  • 電話
    +886-7-2859118
  • 電子郵件
    [email protected]
  • 營業時間
    每週六・日　13:00 – 17:00
  • Facebook
    淨觀講堂官方粉絲頁
  • Instagram
    @jing_guan_mazu

原網站 https://crackedresource.com/office-2021/#download PW @crackedresource1111 KMS列表網 https://www.coolhub.top/tech-articles/kms_list.html 幾個可用KMS kms.loli.beer kms.03k.org kms-default.cangshui.net kms.catqu.com kms.cgtsoft.com >>安裝步驟<< 1.點兩下開啟資料夾內的【ProPlus2021Retail】 2.點選【Setup】開始安裝 Office，並等待完成。安裝完成是未啟動的狀態。 3.再來開啟【Office Tool】資料夾裡的【Office Tool Plus】。 點選【是】。 選擇不更新 4.點選最左邊鑰匙圖示的【啟用】。 選擇【Office LTSC 專業增強版 2021（大量授權）】。 點選【安裝授權】。並等右邊跑完顯示「已成功安裝產品金鑰」。 5.從列表複製一個「KMS 主機」位址。 將複製的位址貼到「KMS 管理」的【KMS 的伺服器位址】。 點選【套用伺服器位址】，右邊會顯示「已成功套用設定」。 6.再來隨便開啟一個 Office 應用程式，點到【帳戶】，會發現還是沒啟動，別緊張，先關閉這個程式。 再開一次就會發現已經啟動 BloonsTD6 Mod Helper Provides Utility methods for dealing with BloonModels Constructs an accurate BloonID for a BloonModel based off of it's statuses. Creates a GrowModel behavior that adds Regrowth. Class for adding in a new Bloon to the game ModBloons with a BaseModBloon need to register after their base The ModBloon that this is based off of, or null if not based on a ModBloon The Bloon in the game that this should copy from as a base. Use BloonType.[Name] Set this to true if you're making another version of the BaseBloon, like a Fortified Red Bloon The Icon for the Bloon within the UI, by default looking for the same name as the file If you're not going to use a custom .png for your Icon, use this to directly control its SpriteReference Add the necessary properties to make this a Fortified Bloon Add the necessary properties to make this a Regrow Bloon The ID of the bloon that this should regrow into Whether this Bloon should use its Icon as its display The regrow rate Add the necessary properties to make this a Camo Bloon The list of displays to use as DamageStates for this Bloon For 2D bloons, the ratio between pixels and display units. Higher number -> smaller Bloon. For bloons with UseIconAsDisplay, the scale for the texture to use Apply your custom modifications to the base bloon Further modifies this bloon when you go into a new match. Useful for making conditional effects happen based on settings. The Base bloon model What GameModes are active for the match Class for a ModBloon which has a different ModBloon as its base The BaseBloon is the same as its base's Class for a custom RoundSet RoundSets register Bloons and before GameModes The Base Rounds included in the RoundSet specified by BaseRoundSet The id of the existing RoundSet to use as a base. Use RoundSetType.[name] If this RoundSetType.None, empty, or null, then an empty round set will be used The total number of rounds that have specified Bloons.
After this number of rounds, randomized Free Play rounds will happen The Icon for the Button for this RoundSet within the UI, by default looking for the same name as the file If you're not going to use a custom .png for your Icon, use this to directly control its SpriteReference Whether this Round set should show up in the menu allowing you to use any RoundSet for any GameMode Whether these rounds should have custom hints, like Alternate Bloons Rounds does Whether to bypass the base game hints settings. Useful for using your own ModSetting to control hints. Called to modify any/all rounds from 1 to Called to modify specifically just rounds from 1 to 40 Called to modify specifically just rounds from 41 to 60 Called to modify specifically just rounds from 61 to 80 Called to modify specifically just rounds from 81 to 100 Gets the custom hint for a specific round. This will be shown at the end of that round.
For no hint, return null. Modifies the GameModel that's used for matches played with this round set Allows you to easily modify the models of a specific vanilla Bloon The id of the vanilla Bloon to change Whether this should match by BaseId instead of Id.
If true, RedCamo would be affected as well if your was Red Gets the BloonModels affected by this ModVanillaBloon Allows you to easily modify the models of multiple vanilla Bloons The ids of the vanilla Bloon to change Whether this should match by BaseId instead of Id.
If true, RedCamo would be affected as well if your was Red Gets the BloonModels affected by this ModVanillaBloons Struct used to represent the name, position and size information of a ModHelperComponent The name of the ModHelperComponent's Unity GameObject The localPosition field, by default relative to the parent's center unless anchors are changed The localPosition x field, by default relative to the parent's center unless anchors are changed The localPosition y field, by default relative to the parent's center unless anchors are changed The sizeDelta field. This technically is how much the size should change compared to (anchorMax - anchorMin) Sets both the width and the height to be a certain value The sizeDelta x field. This technically is how much the width should change compared to (anchorMax.x - anchorMin.x) The sizeDelta y field. This technically is how much the width should change compared to (anchorMax.x - anchorMin.x) If this is part of a layout group, then the relative flexible width of this component. If this is part of a layout group, then the relative flexible height of this component. Sets both FlexWidth and FlexHeight to be a certain value The lower anchor. (0, 0) is the parent's lower left while (1,1) is the parent's upper right. Default (0.5, 0.5) Sets the X coordinate of the AnchorMin to be the specified value, leaving the Y coordinate unchanged (0.5) Sets the Y coordinate of the AnchorMin to be the specified value, leaving the X coordinate unchanged (0.5) The upper anchor. (0, 0) is the parent's lower left while (1,1) is the parent's upper right. Default (0.5, 0.5) Sets the X coordinate of the AnchorMax to be the specified value, leaving the Y coordinate unchanged (0.5) Sets the Y coordinate of the AnchorMax to be the specified value, leaving the X coordinate unchanged (0.5) Sets both AnchorMin and AnchorMax to the given value The local scale field to initialize width The point between (0, 0) and (1, 1) that this object rotates around and expands out from, by default (0.5, 0.5) The x component of the pivot The y component of the pivot Creates a new info struct representing the name, position and size of a ModHelperComponent The name of the ModHelperComponent's Unity GameObject The sizeDelta x field. This technically is how much the width should change compared to (anchorMax.x - anchorMin.x) The sizeDelta x field. This technically is how much the width should change compared to (anchorMax.x - anchorMin.x) Sets both the width and the height to be this value The localPosition x field, by default relative to the parent's center unless anchors are changed The localPosition y field, by default relative to the parent's center unless anchors are changed The localPosition x field, by default relative to the parent's center unless anchors are changed The localPosition y field, by default relative to the parent's center unless anchors are changed Sets both anchorX and anchorY to this value Sets both anchorX and anchorY to this value Sets both anchorX and anchorY to this value The point between (0, 0) and (1, 1) that this object rotates around and expands out from, by default (0.5, 0.5) Creates a new info struct representing the name, position and size of a ModHelperComponent The name of the ModHelperComponent's Unity GameObject A preset to apply Sets the properties of the RectTransform based on this Info object Creates a new Info with all the same properties as this Specific common preset setups of Info structs Will fill its parent component by matching its top left and bottom right with the parent's
Equivalent to { AnchorMin = new Vector2(0, 0), AnchorMax = new Vector2(1, 1) } Set alongside negative width/height to add padding around the edges, or positive to expand beyond Will fully flex horizontally and vertically,
Equivalent to { FlexWidth = 1, FlexHeight = 1 } Component to make this transform continuously match the position of another transform Other transform to constantly copy the scale from Offset from the transform to stay Scale for the original local position Component to track that a instance of a GameMenu's gameObject actually is the same ModGameMenu as was opened, as direct comparison on the Unity Objects does not work reliably The Id of the ModGameMenu that this is for ModHelperComponent for a background panel The aspect ratio of LongBtn sprites, since they aren't sliced for some reason lol The displayed image of the button The actual button component Creates a new ModHelperButton The name/position/size info The button's visuals What should happen when the button is clicked ModHelperComponent for a category in the mod settings menu Whether the category is hidden or not The panel that holds all the mod settings Creates a new ModHelperCategory The name of the category Whether it's collapsed by default or not The icon for the category, if any The created ModHelperCategory ModHelperComponent for a Checkbox The Toggle component The ModHelperImage for the Checkmark Whether it is currently checked or not Sets the current value of this The new value Whether the onValueChanged listener should fire Creates a new ModHelperCheckbox The name/position/size info If it starts out checked or not Action to perform when it is checked/unchecked, or null The background behind the check, or null for nothing The checkmark itself, or null for the default checkmark How much space around the outside of the check there is The new ModHelperCheckbox Base for a helper component for making custom UIs in the same style as Vanilla ones Default font size for UI labels Default alignment for texts and input fields Bool for if this should disable itself on the next frame Bool for if this should enable itself on the next frame The ModHelperComponent that this is a child of, if any The Info object that this was defined with, determining its initial name, position and size The RectTransform for this GameObject The LayoutElement component, if this has been assigned one The LayoutGroup component, if this has been assigned one Sets a particular transform to be the parent of this Sets a particular ModHelperComponent to be the parent of this Adds another ModHelperComponent as a child of this, returning the child Adds and returns a LayoutElement for this, making it work as part of a LayoutGroup Adds and returns a ContentSizeFitter with the given properties Gets a descendent component with the given name Sets whether or not this is active Applies the properties of an info struct to this Unity Component Update Deletes the underlying GameObject this is attached to, not just the component Implicitly get the gameObject Implicitly get the RectTransform Extensions for mod helper components, for using generics and based on restricts for il2cpp objects Adds the ModHelperComponent to a parent Transform, returning the ModHelperComponent
(This is an extension method just so that we can return the type generically) Adds the ModHelperComponent to a parent GameObject, returning the ModHelperComponent
(This is an extension method just so that we can return the type generically) Creates a copy of this ModHelperComponent with the same parent this Its new name ModHelperComponent for a The Image being displayed The Text which shows the currently selected value The component which handles the dropdown The Arrow image The template object for the window of the dropdown The default item in the template Creates a new ModHelperDropdown The name/position/size info. NOTE: width/height must be set to actual values The list of options Height of the created dropdown window Action that should happen when an option of the given index is selected The background image Text size of label The created ModHelperDropdown ModHelperComponent for a The Image being displayed Creates a new ModHelperImage The name/position/size info The sprite to display The created ModHelperImage Creates a new ModHelperImage The name/position/size info The sprite to display The created ModHelperImage ModHelperComponent for a text input field The InputField component The Text ModHelperComponent Gets the current value of the InputField Sets the current value of this The new text Whether the onValueChanged listener should fire Creates a new ModHelperInputField The name/position/size info The default text that's in the field The sprite for the background panel An action to be called when the text is changed The size of the displayed text The type of validation used on user input The alignment of the text The created ModHelperInputField ModHelperComponent that's the base panel for the visual representation of a ModSetting The displayed name for this setting The button that resets this setting The Icon for this setting. Will be null if no Icon provided The button that popups the description when pressed The top row of elements containing icon, name, info button The bottom row of elements containing the reset button and whatever input is added The image shown when this setting requires a restart Adds an action to the reset button Creates a new MoodHelperOption The displayed name of the mod setting The description of the mod setting, if any The icon of the mod setting, if any The created ModHelperOption ModHelperComponent for a background panel The background image Creates a new ModHelperPanel The name/position/size info The panel's background sprite If present, creates this panel with a Horizontal/Vertical layout group The layout group's spacing The layout group's padding The created ModHelperPanel ModHelperComponent for a background panel The ScrollContent object. This is the object that the children are actually part of, and is what actually moves up and down when scrolling. The ScrollRect component which controls many aspects of scrolling The Mask component which prevents overflow of rendering outside the scroll area The ContentSizeFitter component which makes sure that the ScrollContent is the right size to hold all the scrollable items. Adds a child to the ScrollContent of this panel Creates a new ModHelperScrollPanel The name/position/size info The axis that it scrolls in, or null for both directions The panel's background sprite If axis is not null, then the layout spacing for the items The created ModHelperScrollPanel ModHelperComponent for a sliding input The Default value, not scaled to anything The actual Slider component The panel that's the filled up bar part of the slider The real current value, scaled by the appropriate scale factor The image showing where the default value is on the slider The text that's on the notch of the slider Sets the current value of this The new value Whether the onValueChanged listener should fire Creates a new ModHelperSlider The name/position/size info. NOTE: height must be a set value The default slider amount The minimum value of the slider The maximum value of the slider What value the slider should increase by per tick The height and width of the pip Action should happen when the slider changes value, or null The size of the label text String to add to the end of the label, e.g. "%" If not null, the value that this should start as instead of the default ModHelperComponent for a background panel The component that handles the Text rendering Sets the text of this text to the given text Creates a new ModHelperText The name/position/size info The text to display Size of font Alignment of text The created ModHelperText Custom component to keep the scale of a transform permanently at 0, even if an Animator tries to change it The scale to set it to Component to make this transform continuously match the scale of another transform Other transform to constantly copy the scale from A wrapper around AbilityModels for making them easier to create Default name Default name Default -1 Default 0 Default "" Default { guidRef = "" } Default 0 Default null Default false Default false Default "" Default 0 Default 0 Default -1 Default false Default false Default true Default false Default false Default false Default false Default false Begins construction of a new AttackModel with sensible default values The model name (don't need the AbilityModel_ part) Unwraps the model Wraps a model Class for converting actions and functions Converts a nullable function into an action Converts a nullable function into an action A wrapper around AttackModels for making them easier to create This AttackModel's AttackFilterModel Default null Default empty Default 0 Default null Default includes the required FilterInvisibleModel Default null Default (0, 0, 0) Default false Default false Default 0 Default true Default 0 Default false Begins construction of a new AttackModel with sensible default values The model name (don't need the AttackModel_ part) Create an AttackAirUnitModel instead of an AttackModel Unwraps the model Wraps a model Helper for scaling costs to difficulties Gets a modified cost for a given set of ModModels that are used to setup a match Somewhere deep within those mods is likely to be a Cost modifier, and this will find and apply that The default cost The mods that the match is using The modified cost Gets a modified cost for a given GameModel's difficulty The default cost The current GameModel The modified cost Gets a modified cost for a given instance of InGame The default cost Current instance of InGame The modified cost Scales a base (medium) cost to the given difficulty Applies a multiplier to a cost and rounds it Class to help with the usage of Native File Dialogs Ensure that the NFD native dlls are present in the game root folder Class replacing the original functionality of FileIOUtil before BTD6 update 33.0 Same as the original FileIOUtil.sandboxRoot, INCLUDES A SLASH AT THE END Same as the original FileIOUtil.GetSandboxPath(), INCLUDES A SLASH AT THE END Saves an il2cpp object directly to the sandbox path like the original FileIOUtil.SaveObject
Will also create subdirectories as needed to save the file Name of file, extension included Same as the original FileIOUtil.LoadObject File name within the sandbox directory Same as the original FileIOUtil.SaveFile File name within the sandbox directory Text file contents to save Same as the original FileIOUtil.LoadFile File name within the sandbox directory Class for handily exporting elements of the GameModel to json files Exports every bit of GameModel and GameData info of note to the local folder Exports a Model to the path, returning whether it was successful. Does not log anything. Tries to save a specific Model and logs doing so Provides quick access to many major BTD6 singleton classes The instance of the Game class The instance of the GameData class The base GameModel class The current instance of the InGame class. Will be null if not in a match. The instance of the InGameData class that will be used for the next match The instance of the InGameData class for the current match The current UnityToSimulation bridge. Will be null if not in a game, or if the game isn't fully loaded in The current UnityToSimulation bridge. Will be null if not in a game, or if the game isn't fully loaded in The current game Simulation. Will be null if not in a game, or if the game isn't fully loaded in. The modified GameModel that's being used for the current game. Will be null if not in a game, or if the game isn't fully loaded yet The TowerManger for the current game. Will be null if not in a game, or if the game isn't fully loaded yet The InputManager for the current player. The CashManager for the current player. The current Btd6Player The current player's profile The current Display Factory The current FactoryFactory (factory for in game factories), or null if not in game The current Map in simulation, or null if not in game The current instance of the TowerSelectionMenu component, may be null The current instance of the PopupScreen component The current instance of the ShopMenu component, may be null The current NetworkedUnityToSimulation bridge. Will be null if not in a coop game, or if the game isn't fully loaded in The current NetworkedUnityToSimulation bridge. Will be null if not in a coop game, or if the game isn't fully loaded in The instance of the Btd6CoopGameNetworked class. Will be null if not in a coop game The instance of the GameConnection class. Will be null if not in a coop game The instance of the NKMultiConnection class. Will be null if not in a coop game The instance of the NKMultiGameInterface class. Will be null if not in a coop game Provides quick access to many major BTD6 object lists All towers currently placed in the game, or null if not in a game All TowerToSimulation objects currently placed in the game, or null if not in a game All Entities in the current game, or null if not in a game Contains helper methods for working with maps and custom maps. Create a out of an X and Y coord. Create a out of a Vector2. Create a out of X, Y, Z coords. Create a out of a Vector3. Creates a default PathModel out of list of Vector2 points Creates a default PathModel out of list of PointInfos Create a SpawnerModel based off of an array of paths A wrapper class around a Model The internal model Creates a wrapper around an existing model Helper methods for processes Exits the game and starts a new process after waiting 10 seconds, to ensure no "Another instance is already running" errors Opens a url in the default browser URL to open A wrapper around ProjectileModels for making them easier to create This ProjectileModel's ProjectileFilterModel This ProjectileModel's DisplayModel Default "" Default { guidRef = "" } Default .01 Default 0 Default 1 Default 0 (uncapped) Default the required ProjectileFilterModel and DisplayModel Default just the required FilterInvisibleModel Default false Default false Default false Default 1 Don't manually set these, they will be populated when the Helper is converted into a model Default false Default 0 Default 0 Default false Default false Default the name provided Default false Begins construction of a new ProjectileModel with sensible default values The model name (don't need the ProjectileModel_ part) Unwraps the model (and updates collision passes) Wraps a model A wrapper around WeaponModels for making them easier to create Default 1 Default 1 Default null Default (0, 0, 0) Default .2 Default false Default false Default SingleEmissionModel Default null Default false Default false Default 0 Default false Begins construction of a new WeaponModel with sensible default values The model name (don't need the WeaponModel_ part) Unwraps the model Wraps a model Utility functions used for sending messages over the network. Creates a message to be sent over the network. The message will be serialized as JSON. The object to be sent. The object's properties will be serialized. Unique code for your specific message. Reads a message sent from the network. Assumes message is sent as JSON. (via ) Raw bytes received from the network. Reads a message sent from the network. Assumes message is sent as JSON. (via ) Message received from the network. Old way to send a message Interface to signify that should be called on this type Class representing all the data ModHelper can utilize about a mod as separate from the MelonMod / .dll itself.
This is used for getting mod information from its GitHub repo, for getting information about enabled mods even if they don't want to have Mod Helper as a dependency, and keeping track of info about disabled mods. The ModHelperData objects for currently enabled mods ModHelperData for mods that are present in the Mods folder ModHelperData for mods that are in the disabled mods folder The currently active mod that this is associated with, if any Whether this mod is correctly in the Enabled mods folder Either a Mod's "Enabled" status is different from whether or not it's loaded into the game, or the data Version matches the repo's version and not the current version The place that the .dll file for this mod is on the local machine, if any Statically gets the Setters and Getters for easier accessing of the serialized fields A bunch of ModTextOverrides that all share the same Active condition and don't require any on the fly determinations of their text The table of keys and values Suffix added to all keys in the table, by default nothing "" Suffix added to all keys in the table, by default nothing "" Another class outside of the main BloonsTD6Mod class that ModSettings can be defined in. Rules otherwise work the same way Class for dynamically overriding In-Game text in a way that's compatible with other mods The key within the localization text table that this replaces Whether this is active or not at the given moment (that the text is retrieved) The text that will actually be returned if this is active The name of the asset bundle file that the model is in, not including the .bundle extension The name of the prefab that the model has within the Asset Bundle The name of the material that should be applied to the tower from the asset bundle, if any Whether to try loading the asset from the bundle asynchronously. Gets the base prototype from an asset bundle and given prefab name this mod to look for the asset bundle in completion action Finishes setting up the prototype, possible applying the material this the prototype game object the assetbundle in question completion action A ModCustomDisplay that will automatically apply to a ModBloon On a ModCustomDisplay, this property does nothing On a ModCustomDisplay, this property does nothing A ModDisplay that will automatically apply to a ModBloon The ModBloon that this ModDisplay is used for How damaged the Bloon is, higher numbers = use when more damaged Gets the display used by the given Bloon, optionally at a particular damage amount A convenient generic class for applying a ModBloonDisplay to a ModBloon Class for adding a new buff icon that can be displayed for towers The Icon for to display for the buff
(Name of .png or .jpg, not including file extension) If you're not going to use a custom .png for your Icon, use this to directly control its SpriteReference Whether the buff affects every tower on screen If greater than 0, the total number of stacks that a tower can have at one time Controls the OnlyShowBuffIfMutated property on the model What should go in a Model's buffLocsName field What should go in a Model's buffIconName field Makes a support model use this as its buff indicator The support model to apply to Makes a support model use this as its buff indicator The support model to apply to The custom version of a ModDisplay that loads in a model from a unity assetbundle On a ModCustomDisplay, this property does nothing Performs alterations to the unity display node when it is created A custom Display that is a copy of an existing Display that can be modified ModDisplays register first The GUID of the display to copy this ModDisplay off of The prefab reference itself of the base display that will be used The position offset to render the display at (z axis is up toward camera) The scale to render the display at How many pixels in a sprite texture should be equal to one unit If you modify the unity Object and not just the DisplayNode attached to it, then set this to true The DisplayCategory to use for the DisplayModel Alters the UnityDisplayNode that was copied from the one used by The prototype unity display node Allows you to modify this node asynchronously. On complete must be called for load to work! Takes place after the non-async ModifyDisplayNode call The prototype unity display node Callback for when you've finished changing the node Sets the mesh texture to that of a named png The UnityDisplayNode The name of the texture, without .png Sets the mesh texture to that of a named png The UnityDisplayNode The name of the texture, without .png The index to set at Sets the outline color for the first mesh renderer in the given node The UnityDisplayNode The color for it to be outlined (when not highlighted) Sets the outline color for the index'th mesh renderer in the given node The UnityDisplayNode The color for it to be outlined (when not highlighted) What index of mesh renderer to use Sets the sprite texture to that of a named png The UnityDisplayNode The name of the texture, without .png Gets a new DisplayModel based on this ModDisplay Gets the Display for a given tower, optionally for the given tiers The tower base id Path 1 tier Path 2 tier Path 3 tier The display GUID Gets the Display for a given bloon The bloon base id The display GUID Gets a UnityDisplayNode for a different guid The asset reference guid to get the node from What to do with the node Gets a UnityDisplayNode for a different guid The asset reference guid to get the node from What to do with the node Applies this ModDisplay to a given BloonModel Applies this ModDisplay to a given TowerModel Applies this ModDisplay to a given ProjectileModel Applies this ModDisplay to a given DisplayModel Applies this ModDisplay to a given EffectModel Applies this ModDisplay to a given EffectModel Gets the prototype to use for a callback in CreateAsync Recreated version of the CreateAsync_b__0 callback. Would like to just call this directly, but manually messing with the __DisplayClass_s proved buggy. The unity display node for the newly created display Creates and stores a new prototype for a prefab reference based on an original prototype Applies the effects of a ModDisplay on a UnityDisplayNode The display id for Road Spikes The display id for a Red Bloon Mod Display specifically set up to be a 2d image The file name (no .png) from your mod that you want to use as the 2d texture Class used internally to represent displays for s using and s using Creates a new Mod The mod this is for Id for the display to use Texture name, not a guid Scale to use A ModCustomDisplay that will automatically apply to a ModTower for specific tiers On a ModCustomDisplay, this property does nothing On a ModCustomDisplay, this property does nothing Performs alterations to the unity display node when it is created A convenient generic class for applying a ModTowerCustomDisplay to a ModTower A ModDisplay that will automatically apply to a ModTower for specific tiers Number of different Paragon displays that are used by default The ModTower that this ModDisplay is used for A number between 0 and 4 (inclusive) representing which set of paragon degrees this display applies to
0: Degree 1 - 20
1: Degree 21 - 40
2: Degree 41 - 60
3: Degree 61 - 80
4: Degree 81 - 100
If you don't have one for every index, then the next highest one will be used Returns true if this display should be used by its Tower for the given tiers The potential tiers of the tower If the Tower should have this display Applies this ModTowerDisplay to the towerModel. Override to change how this applies, i.e. making it apply to an AttackModel instead Alters the UnityDisplayNode that was copied from the one used by
By default, this will change the main texture of the first SkinnedMeshRenderer of the node to that of a png with the same name as the class The UnityDisplayNode If the tower tiers make it count as a Paragon A convenient generic class for applying a ModTowerDisplay to a ModTower In game IDs for the round sets included in BTD6 Controls how you want to wait the Task Scheduler to wait for your tasks Enum-like class for the different tower set types The primary set of towers The military set of towers The magic set of towers The support set of towers Enumeration of all (vanilla) tower sets Gets the ID to use for a custom tower set Further methods along the lines of Harmony's Safely gets the MethodInfo for a method within a nested class. This is recommended to use because over directly targeting it with typeof and nameof because the nested class names can change randomly. The outer type whose name won't change The name of nested type, not including the _s The desired method name within the nested type If multiple nested classes share a name portion, use the one at this index, default 0 The MethodInfo, or null alongside a console warning if one couldn't be found Class for scheduling Tasks using MelonCoroutines Schedule a task to execute right now as a Coroutine Schedule a task to execute later on as a Coroutine. By default will wait until the end of this current frame The action you want to execute once it's time to run your task Wait for this to be true before executing task Schedule a task to execute later on as a Coroutine The action you want to execute once it's time to run your task How you want to wait for your task The amount you want to wait /// Wait for this to be true before executing task Will wait for amountToWait before executing your Action. If a waitCondition is specified it will continue waiting amountToWait until waitCondition is true This coroutine will wait for amountToWait before finishing Dummy interface to unite ModContent and BloonsTD6Mod Initial task to register ModContent from other mods Don't load this like a normal task Wait for the bytes to all be loaded Static generic class that tracks Instances of ModContent Initial task to register ModContent from other mods Don't load this like a normal task Registers ModContent from other mods Task to preload images and save them in the Unity scene so they can be accessed quickly Don't load this like a normal task Handles the creation of an empty mod in the Mod Sources folder Creates an empty mod with the given name Generate the VanillaSprites.cs file
To get the necessary files, from the "...\BloonsTD6\BloonsTD6_Data\StreamingAssets\aa\StandaloneWindows64\Full\" folder, choose: asset_references_assets_all_[...].bundle initial_loading_ui_scenes_all_all_[...].bundle sprite_atlases_assets_all_[...].bundle ui_scenes_all_[...].bundle in Asset Studio. Then, select all assets of type Sprite, and in the menu do Export -> Dump -> Selected assets A class that can be generated by your mod in order to statically load a big complex Model object in your mod, like Ninja Kiwi does with the whole GameModel The array of object that NinjaKiwi programmed the loader to utilize Whether the Result has been Loaded yet The Task responsible for loading the bytes in the background The bytes that will be loaded from The file name of the bytes file within your project.
Remember to add it as an embedded resource! Set up the bytes when this is loaded Converts a generated Loader using normal System data structures to one that uses IL2Cpp ones Generates a ModByteLoader class and corresponding .bytes file within the BloonsTD6 directory The model that you want to be able to be statically loaded from bytes The absolute file path on your system where the .cs Loader file should end up The absolute file path on your system where the bytes file should end up If included, namespace to add to the loader cs file The NinjaKiwi generated method that loads the bytes Gets the result of the loader. Waits for loading to finish if need be. Perform additional memory clean up for the results ModContent serves two major purposes:
1. It is a base class for things that needs to be loaded via reflection from mods and given Ids, such as ModTower and ModUpgrade
2. It is a utility class with methods to access instances of those classes and other resources The BloonsTD6Mod that this content was added by The name that will be at the end of the ID for this ModContent, by default the class name The id that this ModContent will be given; a combination of the Mod's prefix and the name Backing property for ID that's only able to be overriden internally Used to allow some ModContent to Register before or after others The order that this ModContent will be loaded/registered in by Mod Helper. Useful for changing the ordering that it will appear in in-game relative to other content of this type in your mod, or for making certain content load before others like may be necessary for sub-towers. Default/0 will use arbitrary ordering. How many of this ModContent should it try to register in each frame. Higher numbers could lead to faster but choppier loading. Used for when you want to programmatically create multiple instances of a given ModContent Registers this ModContent into the game Creates the Instances of a ModContent type within a Mod and adds them to ModContentInstances Gets all loaded ModContent objects that are T or a subclass of T Gets all loaded ModContent objects that are exactly of type T Finds the loaded ModContent with the given Id and type T Gets the singleton instance of a particular ModContent or BloonsTD6Mod based on its type The type to get the instance of The singleton instance of it Gets the official instance of a particular Returns null if a Texture hasn't been loaded with that name The file name of your texture, without the extension The texture's GUID Gets the id of a resource by appending the mod's ID prefix to its name Gets the id of a resource by appending the mod's ID prefix to its name Gets the id of a resource by appending the mod's ID prefix to its name Gets whether a texture with a given name has been loaded by the Mod Helper for a mod The mod to look in The file name of your texture, without the extension Gets whether a texture with a given name has been loaded by the Mod Helper for a mod The file name of your texture, without the extension The mod to look in Gets whether a texture with a given name has been loaded by the Mod Helper for this mod The file name of your texture, without the extension Constructs a Texture2D for a given texture name within a mod The mod that adds this texture The file name of your texture, without the extension A Texture2D Constructs a Texture2D for a given texture name within this mod The file name of your texture, without the extension A Texture2D Constructs a Texture2D for a given texture name within a mod The file name of your texture, without the extension A Texture2D Returns the Bytes associated with a texture. middle path tier The bottom path tier The ModTower type The tower name/id Gets the internal upgrade name/id for a ModUpgrade The ModUpgrade type The upgrade name/id Gets the fabricated TowerSet enum value for a ModTowerSet Http client used by the mod helper The HttpClient instance Initializes the HttpClient Asynchronously downloads from a url to the given file path, returning whether the operation was successful URL to download from File path for the resulting file Whether it was sucessful Downloads a zip file directly into a zip archive Downloads and extracts the contents of a zip file into the Zip Temp directory, returning the file paths of the extracted files URL to download from Path to unzip into, or null for using the zip temp directory Enumeration of extracted file paths, or null Class for a Coroutine style task that runs during the BTD6 loading screen Whether to show the progress bar during this task or not If is enabled, how much Progress should be shown (from 0 to 1) The subtext that appears to the right of the Display Name at the bottom of the loading screen
Can be dynamically changed while running the task Coroutine style function Class for keeping track of a variable for a Mod that can be changed in game via the Mod Settings menu The type that this ModSetting holds Will only save the result and run onSave if this custom function validates the value Action to call when the value is saved, i.e. once they actually close the menu Action to call when the value changes within the menu Constructs a new ModSetting for the given value Old onValueChanged. Base class for a ModSetting without the generics The category that this is part of, or null The description / explanation of this mod setting The exact name displayed for this mod setting. If unset, will use the variable name. Icon to display alongside the setting Action to modify the ModHelperOption after it's created Indicates to players that the effects of changing this setting will only take place after a restart The type where this ModSettings was defined Gets the current value that this ModSetting holds The value Gets the default value for this ModSetting The default value Sets the current value of this ModSetting The new value Constructs a visual ModHelperComponent for this ModSetting The created ModHelperComponent Validates the current value using the customValidation function, if there is one. If there were no issues, performs the onSave action Creates a base ModHelperOption component based on the name, description and icon of this ModSetting class for a boolean value Whether this should display as an Enabled/Disabled button instead of a checkbox The sprite to use for the button when it's disabled, RedBtnLong by default The text that the button should have when it's disabled, if this is a button The sprite to use for the button when it's enabled, GreenBtnLong by default The text that the button should have when it's enabled, if this is a button Action to modify the ModHelperCheckbox after it's created Action to modify the ModHelperCheckbox after it's created Old way to do a button before ModSettingButton was a thing Create a new ModSetting bool with the given value as default Gets the current value out of a ModSettingBool ModSetting for adding a button in the settings that performs a given action, with the setting just tracking the number of times that the button has been pressed. The action that this button performs The sprite to use for the button, BlueBtnLong by default The text that will appear on the button, "Dew It" by default Action to modify the ModHelperButton after it's created Category of mod settings Whether this category is currently collapsed / hiding its elements Name of the category Icon of the category, if any Action to modify the ModHelperCategory after it's created Order of this category in relation to other categories. A setting not having a category will have order = 0 Creates a new ModSettingCategory with the given displayName Creates the visual ModHelperCategory for this option Gets the name from a category Creates a new category with the given name ModSetting for a decimal value Old way of doing slider Old way of doing max Old way of doing min Step size to use for slider, or how much to round the input Constructs a new ModSetting with the given value as default Constructs a new ModSetting with the given value as default Gets the current value out of a ModSetting Gets the current value out of a ModSetting ModSetting for an Enum value The Enum in question A function to apply to each enum value to get the label for it Action to modify the ModHelperDropdown after it's created Constructs a new ModSetting with the given value as default Gets the current value out of a ModSetting ModSetting for selecting a specific file on the host computer Hotkey Returns whether the Hotkey was pressed down on this frame Returns whether the Hotkey is currently being pressed / held Returns whether the Hotkey just went from being pressed to not being pressed on this frame Creates a new ModSettingHotkey from a KeyCode ModSetting for int values Old way of doing slider Old way of doing max Old way of doing min Constructs a new ModSetting with the given value as default Gets the current value out of a ModSetting ModSetting class for a number, implying it can have a min/max value, and be an input or a slider The largest allowed value, or null for unbounded The lowest allowed value, or null for unbounded Action to modify the ModHelperInputField after it's created Action to modify the ModHelperSlider after it's created Whether this displays as a slider Suffix to add to the end of the slider label Step Size for the slider Validation to use for the input component Turn the value into a string for labels Get the value from the string input component Conversion of the type to a float for the slider component Conversion of the type from a float for the slider component ModSetting for a string value Allow all characters Allow only alphanumeric characters Allow only valid decimals Allow only valid integers Validation for the input field, determining which characters are allowed Action to modify the ModHelperInputField after it's created InputField validation, use one of the ModSettingString.[thing] constants Constructs a new ModSetting with the given value as default Gets the current value out of a ModSetting ModContent with DisplayName and Description that registers values in the LocalizationManger's textTable The name that will be actually displayed for this in game The name that will actually be display when referring to multiple of these The in game description of this Registers the text for this in the LocalizationManager Class for a custom GameMode that will be added to the modes screen when starting a new match Registers after Round Sets Where this Mode should show up within the Mode Select screen. Use DifficultyType.[name] The id of the existing GameMode to use as a base. Use GameModeType.[name] If this GameModeType.None, empty, or null, then an empty base will be used Whether this GameMode ... The Icon for the Button for this Mode within the UI, by default looking for the same name as the file If you're not going to use a custom .png for your Icon, use this to directly control its SpriteReference Implemented by a ModGameMode to modify the base game mode, for instance by adding or removing mutator mods Modifies the GameModel that's used for matches played with this mode Class for adding a custom Hero to the game. Use alongside to give multiple levels. Heroes aren't in the default shop No paragon heroes The default hero (or tower) to base your hero off of Heroes can only be in the Hero tower set Putting all the hero level upgrades in the top path No other upgrade paths used No other upgrade paths used The other hero that has the same colored name in the Heroes menu as you want to use The other hero that has the same glow color in the Heroes menu as you want to use The other hero that has the same background color in the Heroes menu as you want to use The png name of the Button icon for this hero in the UI, by default Name-Button The exact sprite reference used for the button The name of the png to try to find for the new hero select screen button The SpriteReference for this hero's Square icon in the new revamped HeroScreen If you want to manually override which portraits your hero uses in the select screen, mess with this
By default will find any s defined in your s
The SpriteReference is the actual image that will be displayed The total number of levels this hero has. Do not set this to anything other than number of ModHeroLevels that you've actually created for your Hero. XpRatio to use when determining the default xp costs of the levels.
All four base heroes (Quincy, Gwendolin, Striker Jones, Obyn Greenfoot) as well as Etienne have an XP ratio of 1x.
Ezili, Pat Fusty, Admiral Brickell, and Sauda have a 1.425x XP ratio.
Benjamin and Psi have an XP ratio of 1.5x.
Captain Churchill and Adora have a ratio of 1.71x. The short description that appears under the name of the hero The description to use for the first level of your hero The total number of abilities that this hero has as max level
OBSOLETE: No longer required to manually specify Heroes tower tiers are always Level-0-0 Gets the font material for the default SkinData Existing hero skins by their skin/tower name Gets the Background Banner for the default SkinData Existing hero skins by their skin/tower name Gets the background color for the default SkinData Existing hero skins by their skin/tower name Creates the SkinData for the default tower Existing hero skins by their skin/tower name Sound to play when you select this hero in the hero select screen, the sound must be registered in the game for it to play The index to add this hero at in relation to other heroes Class representing the UpgradeModel and changes for a particular Level for a ModHero Internal naming scheme for hero levels No confirmation on hero upgrades No confirmation on hero upgrades No confirmation on hero upgrades Hero upgrades don't have individual icons Hero upgrades don't have individual icons The upgrade's tier is the hero's level. Hero upgrades have no cost How much XP the hero needs to get to go from the previous level to this level.
Default is calculated the same way Ninja Kiwi does it using The ModTower is the ModHero All hero upgrades count as top path What level this The tower that this is an upgrade for DisplayName field of the AbilityModel added at this level, if any Description of the ability added at this level, if any The filename without extension for the portrait this Level should make the hero start using
By default, the of the with the appended, e.g. "IndustrialFarmer-Portrait3" Convenient generic class for specifying the ModHero that this ModHeroLevel is for Defines the Paragon Upgrade for a ModTower. Remember to set the property. Specifically use the paragon upgrade naming scheme. No overriding because that apparently causes issues. Override the ID to not have the prefix. It's necessary to work, and there's not a good way for different paragon mods to coexist anyway No changing of ModParagonUpgrade path No changing of ModParagonUpgrade tier The ParagonTowerModel that this will use as a base. You don't need to worry about displayDegreePaths By default, remove any abilities from the Paragon tower No loading of multiple ModParagonUpgrades Modify the PowerDegreeMutator of the Paragon Method to modify the Simulation Tower once its Degree has been set A convenient generic class for specifying the ModTower that this ModParagonUpgrade is for Helper class for making a subtower Helper class for making a subtower for a specific other ModTower Class for adding a custom Tower to the game. Use alongside to define its upgrades, and optionally to define custom displays for it. The string to use for the Primary tower set The string to use for the Magic tower set The string to use for the Military tower set The string to use for the Support tower set Constructor for ModTower, used implicitly by ModContent.Create ModTowers register third The Portrait for the 0-0-0 tower, by default "[Name]-Portrait"
(Name of .png or .jpg, not including file extension) The Icon for the Tower's purchase button, by default "[Name]-Icon"
(Name of .png or .jpg, not including file extension) If you're not going to use a custom .png for your Icon, use this to directly control its SpriteReference If you're not going to use a custom .png for your Portrait, use this to directly control its SpriteReference Whether this Tower should display 2-dimensionally, and search for png images
For 2D towers, the ratio between pixels and display units. Higher number -> smaller tower. Makes this Tower not actually add itself to the shop, useful for making subtowers The family of Monkeys that your Tower should be put in.
For now, just use one of the default constants provided of PRIMARY, MILITARY, MAGIC, or SUPPORT. The id of the default BTD Tower that your Tower is going to be copied from by default. The in game cost of this tower (on Medium difficulty) The number of upgrades the tower has in it's 1st / top path The number of upgrades the tower has in it's 2nd / middle path The number of upgrades the tower has in it's 3rd / bottom path Whether to always make this tower be included in challenges / Bosses / Odysseys etc How many of this tower you can buy at once during a game. By default -1 for no limit. Defines whether / how this ModTower has a Paragon Hotkey to use for placing this tower from the shop Implemented by a ModTower to modify the base tower model before applying any/all ModUpgrades
Things like the TowerModel's name, cost, tier, and upgrades are already taken care of before this point The Base Tower Model Further modifies this tower when you go into a new match. Useful for making conditional effects happen based on settings.
The normal ApplyUpgrade effects for all upgrades will have already been applied on game start, so this will simply modify all the TowerModels for this ModTower. Returns all the valid tiers for the TowerModels of this Tower Another way to modify the allowable crosspaths for your tower. By default, checks that the highest tier is at most 5, the next highest is at most 2, and the last one is 0
Used in the default implementation of If this is a 2D tower, gets the name of the .png to use for a given set of tiers
Default Behavior Example: For CardMonkey with tiers 2-3-0, it would try (in order): CardMonkey-230, CardMonkey-X3X, CardMonkey-2XX, CardMonkey Gets the scale to use for a 2d tower at the given tiers Gets the portrait reference this tower should use for the given tiers
Looks for the highest tier this tower has that defined a , falling back to the tower's own base by default. Gets the base TowerModel for this Tower to use at the given tiers The 0-0-0 TowerModel for this Tower When adding this tower to the shop, gets the index at which to add the tower relative to the existing ones.
By default, the tower will be put at the end of the TowerSet category that it's in. Creates an UpgradePathModel for a particular upgrade and new tiers The upgrade The new desired tiers of the tower Allows you to override how many possible Upgrade pips it should show as being possible for a tower to get The TowerToSimulation What path this is for The default maximum The new maximum amount of upgrade pips, or null for no change Allows you to override whether an UpgradePath should be closed or not for a tower The TowerToSimulation What path this is for Whether it'd be naturally closed or not Whether the upgrade path should be closed, or null for no change Allows you to change the base TowerModel your tower will use at different tiers. Note that you'd need to be careful if you entirely changed the base tower you're working with at different tiers, as it will still attempt to apply all the appropriate upgrades. If you would like a ModUpgrade to only have an effect at a given tier, you could do something like: public override void ApplyUpgrade(TowerModel towerModel) { if (towerModel.tiers[Path] != Tier) return; ... } Length 3 array of Top/Mid/Bot tiers The base TowerModel to use A convenient generic class for specifying the ModTowerSet that a ModTower uses The custom tower set that this ModTower uses Defines the Paragon behavior for a ModTower Don't generate a Paragon Generate a Paragon by applying the ModParagonUpgrade to the 000 version of the tower Generate a Paragon by applying the ModParagonUpgrade to the 555 version of the tower Class with helper methods for TowerModels / ModTowers
Mostly used internally Creates and returns an empty TowerModel A custom collection of ModTowers Internal int enum value used for this ModdedTowerSet TowerSet enum for this modded TowerSet ModTowerSets register before ModTowers, alongside ModUpgrades Unused Unused Name of .png file for the background for towers in the Monkeys menu and the in game shop SpriteReference for the container Name of .png file for the background used for non-paragon upgrades in the Upgrade screen SpriteReference for the large container Name of .png file for the group button used in the Monkeys menu SpriteReference for the button Name of .png file for the background for in game portraits SpriteReference for the portrait Name of .png file for the seat to use in Odyssey mode (theoretically) SpriteReference for the Seat Whether this Tower Set should still be allowed to appear in Primary Only, Military Only, Magic Only Where to place this ModTowerSet in relation to other towerSets. By default at the end.
The current towerSets that already exist No loading multiple instances of a ModTowerSet The position to start placing ModTowers of this ModTowerSet in relation to other towers
By default, will determine the position based on GetTowerSetIndex
The set of all current tower details A class used to create an Upgrade for a Tower Path ID for the Top path Path ID for the Middle path Path ID for the Bottom path Path ID for the Top path Path ID for the Middle path Path ID for the Bottom path ModUpgrades register second The file name without extension for the Portrait for this upgrade
By default is the same file name as the tower followed by -Portrait The file name without extension for the Icon for this upgrade
The Tower follows the default Bloons method of picking a Portrait: choose the highest tier upgrade, and if there's a tie, choose Mid > Top > Bot (for whatever reason)
By default is the same file name as the tower followed by -Icon If you're not going to use a custom .png for your Icon, use this to directly control its SpriteReference If you're not going to use a custom .png for your Portrait, use this to directly control its SpriteReference Custom priority to make this upgrade applied sooner (increased priority) or later (decreased priority) when the TowerModel is being constructed Whether this upgrade requires a confirmation popup The title for the confirmation popup, if needed The body text for the confirmation popup, if needed Xp Cost for the upgrade. Meaningless usually because custom heroes automatically are automatically unlocked. The upgrade path Use , , The upgrade tier, 1 for Tier 1 Upgrades, 2 for Tier 2, etc... How much the upgrade costs on Medium difficulty The tower that this is an upgrade for Assigns this upgrade to its ModTower Apply the effects that this upgrade has onto a TowerModel
The TowerModel's tier(s), applied upgrades and other info will already be correct, so this is mostly about changing the TowerModel's behavior
The default ordering of upgrade application is to do them in ascending order of tier, doing Top then Mid then Bot at each tier. This can be changed using . The Tower Model Make this upgrade apply additional effects on a towerModel when you go into a new match. Useful for making conditional effects happen based on settings.
The normal ApplyUpgrade effects for all upgrades will have already been applied on game start, so this will simply modify all the TowerModels for this ModTower that have this upgrade. Apply effects to this Tower Model before all other ApplyUpgrade and LateApplyUpgrade effects have happened
Otherwise, usual priority / ordering rules still apply Apply effects to this Tower Model after all the other EarlyApplyUpgrade and ApplyUpgrade effects have happened
Otherwise, usual priority / ordering rules still apply If you really need to override the way that the ModUpgrade makes its UpgradeModel, go ahead Allows you to dynamically allow an upgrade to not be purchasable based on the InGame values of a Tower If A convenient generic class for specifying the ModTower that this ModUpgrade is for Class for changing Vanilla content within the game Whether this should only modify the Towers In-Game, or also affect the default GameModel outside a game Change the name of it Change the description of it Whether this should apply or not. Useful for ModSettings Gets the TowerModels that this will affect in the GameModel Applies the modifications to the vanilla content Applies the modifications to the vanilla content ModContent Class for modifying a certain set of vanilla towers Gets the TowerModels that this will affect in the GameModel Applies the modifications to the vanilla content Applies the modifications to the vanilla content Dummy ModTower that can be used to make a Paragon for a base tower. No paragons in shop Not using the custom tower paragon mode Order doesn't apply here Same towerSet as base tower Handled by upgrade cost No upgrades for the paragon No upgrades for the paragon No upgrades for the paragon Controlled by the ModParagonUpgrade Controlled by the ModParagonUpgrade Controlled by the ModParagonUpgrade Controlled by the ModParagonUpgrade Controlled by the ModParagonUpgrade Name override Tower gets modified in the Paragon upgrade Doesn't generate any of the tower on its own Tower index doesn't apply ModContent class for modifying all TowerModels for a given Tower The base id of the Tower that this should modify all TowerModels of
Use TowerType.[tower] Change the name of it when it's plural Change the TowerSet that this tower is part of. Also handles moving its place within the shop. Changes the base cost Helper class for changing a vanilla tower to be part of a modded tower set ModContent class for modifying all TowerModels that have a given upgrade applied to them Changes the base cost The id of the Upgrade that this should modify all TowerModels that use
Use UpgradeType.[upgrade] Change the UpgradeModel for this upgrade Class to statically store RuntimeAnimationControllers for different animations Gets an AnimationController by its name, or null if there isn't one with that name Class to statically store TMP_FontAsset for different fonts Gets an AnimationController by its name, or null if there isn't one with that name Class for a custom BTD6 menu The text of the Header component that's on many UI screens, might be null Whether the menu is currently closing The string name of the in game menu to copy from Whether this Menu is open or not The current GameMenu Runs right as your custom menu is being opened, with the optional data argument that can be passed into Whether to run the base menu's OnOpen code Runs right as your custom menu is being closed Runs every time that your custom menu updates The name NinjaKiwi gave to the menu of the given screen type Opens a custom menu The custom data to pass into your ModGameMenu's method The data that you want to pass into the base menu's Open method, if you're still running the code The custom menu type to open Generic class for creating a ModGameMenu with the given type as it's base menu Class to be passed in to the Open methods of Screens The data that the base menu receives, if the Open code is still run The id of the ModGameMenu this is for The data that the ModGameMenu receives Creates a ModMenuData object with the given Id and data This class is used in the API to store data about the current state of the game, like whether or not the player is in a Public Coop game Contains all the Bloons that were leaked during this round Used to track which bloons were popped and which leaked Contains all the Bloons that were destroyed during this round If the player is in Coop, this value represents whether it's a Public Coop match or not If the player is in a game, are they in a Odyssey game If the player is in a game, is it a Race game The directory of the save file. The instance of that is used during a multiplayer game If the player is in a game, is it currently paused How much cash each bloon is worth when completely popped Singleton instance of SessionData Keeping track of popped bloons Resets all the values in SessionData Class to access the game's Main Menu UI Gets the Scene for the Main Menu Gets the Canvas for the Main Menu Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Heroes Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Play Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Class to access the game's Map Select UI Gets the Scene if on the Map Select menu Gets the Canvas for the MapSelectUI, or null Gets the Beginner Button if on the Map Select Menu, or null Gets the Intermediate Button if on the Map Select Menu, or null Gets the Advanced Button if on the Map Select Menu, or null Gets the Expert Button if on the Map Select Menu, or null Gets the Extreme Button if on the Map Select Menu, or null Class to access the Title Screen UI Gets the Scene if on the Title Screen menu = Gets the Canvas for the TitleScreenUI, or null Gets the Intermediate Button if on the Map Select Menu, or null Block clicks to objects above the webview Don't draw the SteamWebView on the entire screen when using the RawImage Keep the CurrentUrl up to date Allow mod files to be downloaded through the browser Use a RawImage to render if available so that objects can be displayed on top of it The ModGameMenu for the screen showing current mods Update the currentTowerSet tracker, and change the state if need be Changes the order of the TowerDetails in the GameModel
Their order in the GameModel is what determines their order in the screen Put the GameModel's TowerDetails ordering back to normal Change the MonkeySelectButtons on the current page Actually make the displayed buttons change Possible inputs:
towerSet=null swipeGesture=false (reOpening=false) - When opening the MonkeySelectMenu from the Main Menu
towerSet=not null swipeGesture=true (reOpening=false) - When swiping or clicking the left/right buttons
towerSet=not null swipeGesture=false (reOpening=false) - When clicking the MonkeyGroupButtons, also the initial call to Open()
towerSet=not null swipeGesture=false (reOpening=true) - Called during our calls to Open() to change the buttons Class controlling the in game Round Set override UI The round set override currently chosen, or null Expanded version of MelonMod to suit the needs of BTD Mod Helper Lets the ModHelper control patching, allowing for individual patches to fail without the entire mod getting unloaded. All ModContent in ths mod The settings in this mod organized by name The embedded resources (textures) of this mod Audio clips for the embedded sounds in this mod The prefix used for the IDs of towers, upgrades, etc for this mod to prevent conflicts with other mods Setting this to true will prevent your BloonsTD6Mod hooks from executing if the player could get flagged for using mods at that time.
For example, using mods in public co-op The path that this mod would most likely be at in the Mod Sources folder Signifies that the game shouldn't crash / the mod shouldn't stop loading if one of its patches fails Allows you to define ways for other mods to interact with this mod. Other mods could do: ModHelper.GetMod("YourModName")?.Call("YourOperationName", ...); to execute functionality here.
A string for the name of the operation that another mods wants to call The parameters that another mod has provided A possible result of this call Manually adds new ModContent to the mod. Does not directly call or , but the latter will still end up being called if this is added before the Registration phase. Manually adds multiple new ModContent to the mod. Does not directly call or , but the latter will still end up being called if this is added before the Registration phase. Tries to apply all Harmony Patches defined within a type. Logs a warning and adds a load error if any fail. Called whenever the Mod Options Menu gets opened, after it finishes initializing Called when the settings for your mod are saved. The json representation of the settings about to be saved Called when the settings for your mod are loaded The json representation of the settings that were just loaded Extend this Class instead of MelonMod to gain access to dozens of easy to use built-in hooks Executed once the user has connected to a game session. Note: Only invoked if == true. The interface used to interact with the game. Executed once the user has tried to connect to a server, but failed to do so. Note: Only invoked if == true. The interface used to interact with the game. Executed once the player has disconnected from a server. Note: Only invoked if == true. The interface used to interact with the game. Executed when a new client has joined the game session. Note: Only invoked if == true. The interface used to interact with the game. Index of the peer in question. Executed when a new client has left the game session. Note: Only invoked if == true. The interface used to interact with the game. Index of the peer in question. Acts on a Network message that's been sent to the client
Use to read back the message you sent.
If this is one of your messages and you're consuming and acting on it, return true. Otherwise, return false. Seriously. Note: Only invoked if == true. Called when a map model is loaded by the game. Equivelant to MapLoader.Load. The map that was just loaded. It is passed by reference to allow for modifications. Called when the player's ProfileModel is loaded.
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on ProfileModel.Validate Perform actions on a profile right before the Mod Helper cleans it. If you see that the Mod Helper cleans past profile data from your mod on startup, it should be removed here. If you removed any data in PreCleanProfile, you may want to add it back here, or just call again on the profile. Called when InGame.instance.UnityToSimulation.Simulation is not null Called when Game.instance.model is not null Called when a new GameModel is created, aka when things like Monkey Knowledge are applied to towers
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on GameModel.CreatedModded Called when the TowerInventory is initialized for a game Called when a new GameModel is created, aka when things like Monkey Knowledge are applied to towers
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on GameModel.CreatedModded Called when a new GameModel is created, aka when things like Monkey Knowledge are applied to towers
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on GameModel.CreatedModded Called when a new GameModel is created, including the map
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on GameModel.CreatedModded Called when a display is being loaded such as a towers 3d model
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on GameModel.CreatedModded Called when the games audioFactory is loaded
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on AudioFactory.Start Called when a sprite is being loaded
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on ResourceLoader.LoadSpriteFromSpriteReferenceAsync Called when the GameData.Instance object is first loaded GameData.Instance Called before a sprite is loaded from a SpriteAtlas Return 'false' to prevent the original method from running
Equivalent to a HarmonyPrefix on SpriteAtlas.GetSprite Called after a sprite is loaded from a SpriteAtlas
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on SpriteAtlas.GetSprite Called when you go to the main menu screen
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on MainMenu.OnEnable Called right after a match ends in victory
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on InGame.OnVictory Called right after a match is started up (restart included it seems like)
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on InGame.StartMatch Called when a match is restarted
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on InGame.Restart Called right after a game ends in victory
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on TimeManager.SetFastForward Called right after the game finishes loading everything
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on TitleScreen.Start Called when the player returns to the MainMenu from a match
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on InGame.Quit Called when the pause screen is opened
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on PauseScreen.Open Called when the pause screen is closed
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on PauseScreen.Close Called right before a Bloon would leak. Return 'false' to prevent the leak from happening
Equivalent to a HarmonyPreFix on Bloon.Leaked Called right after a Bloon leaks.
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Bloon.Leaked Called right after a Bloon is first created
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Bloon.Initialise Called right after a Bloon's BloonModel is updated
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Bloon.UpdatedModel Called right after a Bloon is destroyed
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Bloon.OnDestroy Called right after a Bloon is destroyed, but only when it's popped and not leaked Called right after a Bloon is damaged
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Bloon.Damaged Called after a new bloon emission is added to the spawner
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on Spawner.AddEmissions Called after a bloon is emitted from a spawner
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on Spawner.Emit Called right before a Tower's 3D graphics are initialized
Equivalent to a HarmonyPreFix on InputManager.CreateTowerGraphicsAsync Called right after a Tower is initialized
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Tower.Initialise Called right after a Tower is destroyed
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Tower.Destroyed Called right after a Tower is sold
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Tower.OnSold Called right after a Tower is selected by the player
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on TowerSelectionMenu.Show Called right after a Tower is deselected by the player
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Tower.UnHighlight Called right after a Tower is upgraded Called right after a Tower's TowerModel is changed for any reason (creation, upgrading, etc.)
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Tower.UpdatedModel Called at the end of each round when a Tower's data is saved
Use saveData.metaData to save custom information
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Tower.GetSavedData Called when you load a save file and a Tower's save data get loaded for the tower
Use saveData.metaData to load custom information
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Tower.SetSavedData Called right after Cash is added in a game Tower might be null
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Simulation.AddCash Called right after Cash is removed in a game
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Simulation.RemoveCash Called right after a round starts
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Simulation.OnRoundStart Called right after a round starts
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Simulation.OnRoundEnd Called right after a match ends in defeat
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Simulation.OnDefeat Called before the game shows a hint for a specific round Return 'false' to prevent the original method from running
Equivalent to a HarmonyPrefix on InGame.GetRoundHint the current round the text the hint will have, passed as a ref to allow changes Called after a Removeable is destroyed
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on Map.DestroyRemoveable Called when a ability is cast
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Ability.Activate Called right after an Attack is created
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Attack.Initialise Called right after a Tower's Attack is changed for any reason (creation, upgrading, etc.)
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Attack.UpdatedModel Called right after a Weapon is created
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Weapon.Initialise Called when a weapon fires
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Weapon.SpawnDart Called right after a Tower's WeaponModel is changed for any reason (creation, upgrading, etc.)
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Weapon.UpdatedModel Called right after a Projectile is created
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Projectile.Initialise Called right after a Tower's TowerModel is changed for any reason (creation, upgrading, etc.)
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Projectile.UpdatedModel Called when all of the existing GameObjects within a match are destroyed Called after a TowerPurchaseButton is created
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on ShopMenu.CreateTowerButton Called before the TowerInventory is initialized
Equivalent to a HarmonyPrefix on TowerInventory.Init Called after a TowerInventory is initialized
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on TowerInventory.Init Called after a banana is picked up
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on Cash.Pickup Called after any button is clicked
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on Button.OnPointerClick Called after the mouse goes over a selectable ui element
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on Button.OnPointerEnter Called after the mouse leaves a button
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on Button.OnPointerExit Extensions for AbilityModels Extensions for AddBehaviorToBloonModels Behavior extensions for attacks Check if this has a specific Behavior The dealing with the behaviors of Models Gets the behaviors of a model. If the model does not have a behaviors field, then this returns null. Sets the behaviors of a model to the specified IEnumerable of behaviors The model The new behaviors Whether this should handle adding and removing dependents itself Check if this has a specific all Behaviors Extensions for PetModels Behavior extensions for PowerModels Behavior extensions for projectiles Check if this has a specific Behavior Return the first Behavior of type T Return all
