the first Behavior of type T The Behavior you want to remove Remove all Behaviors of type T The Behavior you want to remove Extensions for BloonsTD6Mods (for some reason lol) Get the name of this mod from it's dll name Get the personal mod directory for this specific mod. Useful for keeping this mod's files seperate from other mods. Example: "BloonsTD6/Mods/BloonsTD6 Mod Helper/settings.txt" Get the personal mod directory for this specific mod. Useful for keeping this mod's files seperate from other mods. Example: "BloonsTD6/Mods/BloonsTD6 Mod Helper/settings.txt" Create the mod's directory if it doesn't exist yet? Gets the directory where this mod's settings are or will be stored. Example: "BloonsTD6/Mods/BloonsTD6 Mod Helper/settings.txt" Gets the directory where this mod's settings are or will be stored. Example: "BloonsTD6/Mods/BloonsTD6 Mod Helper/settings.txt" Create the mod's directory if it doesn't exist yet? Extensions for arrays Version of TryCast without the generic restriction Checks if the parameter array has the given types Checks if the parameter array has the given types Checks if the parameter array has the given types Checks if the parameter array has the given types Checks if the parameter array has the given types Checks if the parameter array has the given types Performs the specified action on each element Action to preform on each element Retrieves all the elements that match the conditions defined by the specified predicate. The Predicate delegate that defines the conditions of the elements to search for. Return whether or not there are any elements in this Return whether or not there are any elements in this that match the predicate Return as Il2CppSystem.List Return as Il2CppReferenceArray Return as LockList Return a duplicate of this Array Return a duplicate of this array as type TCast The original Array Type The Cast type Return this with an Item added to it The Type of the Item you want to add Item to add to this Return this with Items added to it The Type of the Items you want to add Items you want to add Return this with Items added to it The Type of the Items you want to add Items you want to add Check if this has any items of type TCast The Type you're checking for Return the first Item of type TCast The Type of the Item you want Return all Items of type TCast The Type of the Items you want Return this with the first Item of type TCast removed The Type of the Item you want to remove Return this with the first Item of type TCast removed The Type of the Item you want to remove The specific Item to remove Return this with all Items of type TCast removed The Type of the Items that you want to remove A string with all array elements printed together with no spaces
Useful for the suffix for Tower IDS like DartMonkey-230 The array Returns the index of the highest tier, then the middle, then the lowest
Breaks ties by Middle Path >> Top Path >> Bottom Path Returns whether an int array is a valid set of tiers for a Tower Extensions for the normal System IEnumerable class Return as Il2CppSystem.List Return as Il2CppReferenceArray Return as LockList Casts a reference array to an IEnumerable of a different Il2cpptype.
Objects that aren't of the specified type will end up as null in the result Returns the argument that maximizes the given value Deconstruct IGrouping to list Repeats each element in the sequence n times, keeping the same order of elements Filters the elements of the IEnumerable by if their Il2Cpp type is T The Il2Cpp type to filter by Extensions for il2cpp ienumerables Get the IEnumerator as type Il2CppSystem.Collections.IEnumerator. Needed for IEnumerator.MoveNext(). with all Items of type TCast removed The Type of the Items that you want to remove Extensions for sending and receiving data in coop Returns true if the player is a host in a co-op game. Works for both lobby and in-game. Send a Message to all players in the lobby Message to send Convert an object to json and send it players or a player in the lobby Object you want to send. The properties of the object will be serialised as JSON. The id of the peer you want the message to go to. Leave null if you want to send to all players Coop code used to distinguish this message from others. Like a lock and key for reading messages Send a string to players or a player in the lobby string message to send. Can be JSON The id of the peer you want the message to go to. Leave null if you want to send to all players Coop code used to distinguish this message from others. Like a lock and key for reading messages Convert messageBytes to an object of type T Type to convert bytes to messageBytes Convert a Message's bytes to an object of type T Type to convert bytes to Message you want to read Convert an object to json and send it players or a player in the lobby Object you want to send. The properties of the object will be serialised as JSON. The id of the peer you want the message to go to. Leave null if you want to send to all players Coop code used to distinguish this message from others. Like a lock and key for reading messages Adding more deconstruct methods to things Deconstruct a rectangle For some reason the normal deconstruct isn't accessible in all places? For some reason the normal deconstruct isn't accessible in all places? Extension for dumping textures from UnityDisplayNodes Only runs when tower placed. Dumps any textures inside of a display node into Ninja Kiwi directory Extensions for Functions Convert a function to an action Convert a function to an action Extensions for Game Returns the instance of the Map Loader. Returns a new SpriteReference that uses the given guid Returns whether or not the player's account is currently flagged/hackerpooled Get the instance of LocalizationManager Schedule a task to execute right now as a Coroutine Schedule a task to execute later on as a Coroutine. By default will wait until the end of this current frame The action you want to execute once it's time to run your task Wait for this to be true before executing task Schedule a task to execute later on as a Coroutine The action you want to execute once it's time to run your task How you want to wait for your task The amount you want to wait /// Wait for this to be true before executing task Get Player LinkAccount. Contains limited info about player's NinjaKiwi account Get the ProfileModel for the Player Get the PlayerService for the player Get GameModel. Same as using Game.instance.model Uses custom message popup to show a message in game. Currently only works in active game sessions and not on Main Menu the Game instance Message body Message title. Will be mod name by default Uses custom message popup to show a message in game. Currently only works in active game sessions and not on Main Menu the Game instance Message body Time to show message on screen Message title. Will be mod name by default Returns the current lobby screen. Returns the current lobby connection. Returns the directory where the Player's Profile.save file is located. Not set until after reaching the Main Menu for the first time Makes a save of Player.Save at the specified path Path to save to Checks if Player is in a game mode that would get them flagged if using mods Returns if Player is in a race Returns if Player is in a public co-op match Returns if Player is in a Odyssey game Get nkGI for the current session. Will be null if not in multiplayer game or lobby Get the Unity Display Factory that manages on screen sprites. This Factory is different from other Factories in the game Get the instance of PopupScreen Get the instance of ShopMenu Get the instance of CommonForegroundScreen Get the instance of CommonBackgroundScreen Get the instance of MenuManager Get the instance of UI Not tested Get the Btd6Player data for the player. Contains different info than PlayerProfile Get all TowerDetailModels Get all HeroDetailModels Get all PowerDetailModels Get player's current Monkey Money amount Add Monkey Money to player's total Monkey Money the Game instance Amount to add Set player's Monkey Money amount the Game instance Value to set Monkey Money to Extensions for Harmony stuff Add a prefix patch Add a postfix patch Extensions for Il2cpp delegates Create a new and seperate copy of this object. Same as using: .Clone().Cast(); Type of object you want to cast to when duplicating. Done automatically Extensions for il2cpp dictionaries Get all of the values from this Dictionary as a list Deconstruct method of IL2CPP KeyValuePairs Extensions for Il2cpp objects Is this Reference equal to another Object's Reference? Object to compare to Check if object is the same type as T Type to check Check if object is the same type as T The casted object if this is of type T Type to check Gets the exact il2cpp type name of an object Box a float into an Il2cpp object Box a int into an Il2cpp object Box a bool into an Il2cpp object Extensions for the InGame class Returns whether or not the player is currently in a game. Get the current Map Get the current Simulation for this InGame session The Game.model that is being used for this InGame.instance Get the main Factory that creates and manages all other Factories Get the Factory for a specific Type. Ex: Getting the Factory that makes Towers Get every Tower that has been created through the Tower Factory Optionally only get Towers whose TowerModel name is this parameter Get all TowerToSimulations /// Optionally only get Towers whose TowerModel name is this parameter Get's all Bloons on the map Get's all existing BloonToSimulations Get's all existing Projectiles on the map Get the current TowerManager for this game session Get's all AbilityToSimulations currently in the game Get's the UnityToSimulation for this game Gets all objects of type T. Does this by returning all objects created by the Factory of type T The type of items you want Sells multiple towers Sells a tower Returns true if the initial co-op handshake has finished and user has co-op game details. The game. Custom API method that changes the game's round set to a custom RoundSetModel. New Round Set Model to use Get the game object that owns all InGame UI elements Get the Cash Manager for the current game InGame instance Index of the cash manager. Default is 0 Get the Player's current cash Add cash to the Player's wallet InGame instance Amount of cash to add to player wallet Set the Player's cash to a specific amount InGame instance Value to set cash to Get the Player's current health Add health to the players current health InGame instance Amount of health to add Set player's health to specific amount InGame instance Value to set health to Get the player's max health Add to the player's max health InGame instance Amount to add to the player's max health Set the player's maximum health to a new value InGame instance Value to set max health to Get collection of popped bloons in this game. Right now only works for current games. Does not store results from loaded games Get the current instance of TowerInventory being used in this game session Set the current round Spawn bloons in game Spawn bloons in game Spawn bloons in game Add a weapon to this Attack Model this Weapon to add Remove a weapon from this Attack Model this Weapon to remove Sets the weapon at the given index (default 0) of this attack model to be the given one. this Weapon to add Index within weapons array thrown when index < 0 Recursively get all ProjectileModels for this attack model and all of it's weapons Applies the given ModDisplay to the index'th (or first) DisplayModel in the behaviors of the AttackModel.
If there are no DisplayModels, then this does nothing Adds a new filter to this projectile model Removes a specific filter from this attack model Removes the first filter of a given type from this attack model Extensions for BloonModels (Cross-Game compatable) Return how much cash this bloon would give if popped by number of layers How many layers of bloons to pop, ignoring layer health. If less than 0, calculates for the entire bloon Return the number position of this bloon from the list of all bloons (Game.instance.model.bloons) Spawn this BloonModel on the map right now Create a BloonEmissionModel from this BloonModel Number of bloons in this emission model Space between each bloon in this emission model Return all BloonToSimulations with this BloonModel Return all BloonToSimulations with this BloonModel Set the Display GUID for this BloonModel. Returns the Display GUID for this BloonModel. Return the Base ID of this BloonModel Returns whether or not this BloonModel is a Camo bloon. Set whether or not this BloonModel is a Camo bloon. Returns whether or not this BloonModel is a Regrow bloon. Set whether or not this BloonModel is a Regrow bloon. Adds the Regrow behavior to this BloonModel and sets what Bloon it Regrows into. Set whether or not this BloonModel is a Fortified bloon. Returns whether or not this BloonModel is an MOAB-Class bloon. Set whether or not this BloonModel is a Fortified bloon. Applies a given ModDisplay to this TowerModel The type of ModDisplay Adds a child to be spawned from the Bloon Gets the SpawnChildrenModel for the bloon, and optionally creates one if it doesn't exist Adds a child to be spawned from the Bloon Removes all spawned children from this BloonModel Removes up to amount of the given Bloon from the spawned children Replaces all spawned child Bloons with the given id with the given ModBloon Replaces all spawned child Bloons that have id oldId with the given ModBloon Replaces all spawned child Bloons of the first ModBloon type with the second ModBloon type Finds the id for a bloon that has the properties of this bloonModel, or null if there isn't one Makes all children of this Bloon Camo, if they can have it Makes all children of this Bloon Regrow, if they can have it Makes all children of this Bloon Fortified, if they can have it Gets the ModBloon associated with this BloonModel
If there is no associated ModBloon, returns null Adds a tag to the BloonModel, if it doesn't already exist Removes a tag from the BloonModel, if it exists Gets the BloonModel for this group Extensions for DisplayModel Applies a given ModDisplay to this DisplayModel The type of ModDisplay Extensions for the GameModel Returns whether or not any TowerModels in have in it's name Returns whether or not any TowerDetailsModels in have in it's name Add a TowerModel to the game.
Using this method is preferable than modifying the GameModel's towers list manually, as this does more things to more fully integrate the tower within the game the GameModel instance TowerModel to add Optionally add a TowerDetailsModel for your towerModel Add multiple TowerModels to the game more efficiently than calling the single method repeatedly. Adds a tower Adds a TowerDetailsModel to the GameModel's TowerSet, taking into account what set of towers it's a part of
For example, a new custom Primary tower would be added right at the end of the primary towers, and right before the start of the military towers The GameModel The TowerDetailsModel to be added The TowerSet of the tower to be added Adds a TowerDetailsModel to the GameModel's TowerSet at a particular index Adds a HeroDetailsModel to the GameModel's HeroSet at a particular index Return all TowerDetailModels Return all ShopTowerDetailModels Return all TowerModels with a specific base id the GameModel instance The base id all towers should share. Example: "DartMonkey" Get a TowerModel from model.towers. NOTE: model.GetTower cannot get custom towers so use this method instead Create a BloonEmissionModel from a bloonModel the GameModel instance The bloon model that these bloons should be Number of Bloons in this emission Space between each bloon in this emission Create a BloonEmissionModel from a bloon's name the GameModel instance Name of bloon. Example: "Red" Number of Bloons in this emission Space between each bloon in this emission Create a single BloonEmission the GameModel instance Name of this bloon. Example: "Red" Time the bloon should be spawned Create a single BloonEmission the GameModel instance Name of this bloon. Example: "Red" Time the bloon should be spawned Creates a BloonGroup Return all AttackModels from every TowerModel in the game Return all WeaponModels from every AttackModel in the game Return all ProjectileModels from every TowerModel in the game Return all AbilityModels from every TowerModel in the game Return all TowerModels that have at least one AbilityModel Fully adds an UpgradeModel to the GameModel Fully adds multiple UpgradeModels to the GameModel Fully adds multiple UpgradeModels to the GameModel Returns the first TowerDetailsModel in that has a towerId of The you are searching for The first TowerDetailsModel found, otherwise returns null Returns whether or not a bloon exists with this name Extension methods for . Sets which bloon this should regrow into. The ID of the bloon this should regrow into Sets which bloon this should regrow into. The ID of the bloon this should regrow into The rate at which this regrows. Returns the ID of the BloonModel that this regrows into. Other miscellaneous extensions for various Model classes Applies the given ModDisplay to this effect Applies the given ModDisplay to this asset path Gets the damageMultiplier field for a DamageModifierModel Updates the to be consistent with the and Extensions for Models Create a new and separate copy of this object. Same as using: .Clone().Cast(); Type of object you want to cast to when duplicating. Done automatically Turns any descendants with "_" as their name into unique named models Finds the first descendent of a given type whose name contains the specified string, or null if not found Finds the first descendent of a given type that matches the given condition, or null if not found Finds the descendents of a given type whose name contains the specified string Finds the descendents of a given type that matches the given condition Finds the descendents of a given type Sets the name of this model, properly appending the type prefix to it.
Also sets the id if it's a ProjectileModel The model itself Duplicates a model and also sets its name to something new Type of object you want to cast to when duplicating. Done automatically Remove multiple Child Dependants at the same time. Equivalent to calling the (weirdly non plural named) Model.RemoveChild method but without having to do an ICollection cast Add multiple Child Dependants at the same time. Equivalent to calling the (weirdly non plural named) Model.AddChild method but without having to do an ICollection cast Returns whether a model has a descendant of a given type Returns whether a model has a descendant of a given type, providing it via the out parameter if so Returns whether a model has a descendant of a given type with a filtered name, providing it via the out parameter if so Extensions for the ModModel (GameMode) class Check if this has a specific Mutator The Mutator you're checking for Check if this has a specific Mutator and return it The Mutator you're checking for Return the first Mutator of type T, or null if there isn't one The Mutator you want Return the index'th Mutator of type T, or null The Mutator you want Return the first Mutator of type T whose name contains the given string, or null The Mutator you want Return all Mutators of type T The Mutator you want Add a Mutator to this ModModel Remove the first Mutator of Type T The Mutator you want to remove Remove the index'th Mutator of Type T The Mutator you want to remove Remove the first Mutator of Type T whose name contains a certain string The Mutator you want to remove Removes a specific mutator from a tower Remove all Mutators of type T Removes all mutators that match a given condition Makes this GameMode use the given RoundSet Makes this GameMode use the given RoundSet Sets the cash this mode starts you with using a If not 0, the new base cash amount to set the starting amount to How much cash to add to the default base cash If not 0, an overall multiplier to the amount of starting cash Sets the life total this mode starts you with using a Sets the maximum life total this mode starts you with using a Sets the round this mode starts at using a Sets the round this mode ends at using a Sets whether Monkey Knowledge is enabled for a gamemode Sets whether Powers are enabled for a game mode Sets whether Continues are enabled for a game mode Sets whether selling towers is enabled for a game mode Sets whether extra income is enabled for a game mode Modifies the Health that Bloons with a given tag have, like for all Moabs The multiplier to apply to Bloons' health The Bloon tag to apply to Prevents a particular TowerSet from being used in this mode The tower set to lock Whether to lock or unlock the tower set Sets the max health and shield amount to 1 Sets whether the Bloons go in reverse Applies a multiplier to all cash gains in the mode (but not starting cash) Sets the portion of cash that should be gotten back when selling (0.7 by default) Extensions for ProfileModels Add a tower to the list of UnlockedTowers The ID of the tower you want to unlock Add a tower to the list of UnlockedTowers only if the TowerModel is in Game.instance.model.towers The ID of the tower you want to unlock If set to true the TowerModel will only be unlocked if it has been registered in the game Returns whether or not the tower was unlocked Extensions for ProjectileModels Get the DamageModel behavior from the list of behaviors Get all Projectile Simulations that have this ProjectileModel Applies a given ModDisplay to this ProjectileModel The type of ModDisplay Returns whether a projectile is able to hit Camo bloons Makes a projectile model able to see Camo or not ] Adds a new filter to this projectile model Removes a specific filter from this projectile model Removes the first filter of the given type from this projectile model Sets the display for this projectile Sets the display for this projectile Extensions for RoundModels Adds a new group of Bloons to this round The round model The id of the Bloon How many Bloons will be emitted When this group starts emitting, in frames (seconds / 60) When this group stops emitting, in frames (seconds / 60) Adds a new group of Bloons to this round The round model How many Bloons will be emitted When this group starts emitting, in frames (seconds / 60) When this group stops emitting, in frames (seconds / 60) Removes all Bloon Groups from the Round Removes all Bloon Groups where the id is as specified Removes the index'th Bloon Group where the id is as specified Replaces BloonGroups of a certain bloonId with ones for a new Id Replaces BloonGroups of a certain bloonId with ones for a new Id Extension methods for support models Makes a support model use a particular ModBuffIcon as its display The support model to apply to The ModBuffIcon type Extensions for TowerDetailsModels Gets the index of this TowerDetailsModel within the GameModel Returns if this TowerDetailModel is actually for a Hero Get the TowerPurchaseButton that is used to buy this specific TowerDetailModel Get the ShopTowerDetails for this TowerDetailModel Makes a copy of this TowerDetailsModel with a new name Makes a copy of this TowerDetailsModel with a new name and index Sets the name of this TowerDetailsModel, following the naming convention of ofther TowerDetailModels. Example, using "NewMonkey" will set the name to "TowerDetailsModel_NewMonkey" Gets the TowerModel for this TowerDetailsModel Extensions for TowerModels Get the name of the BaseTower. Will be different from this TowerModel's name if this TowerModel isn't a BaseTower Has player already unlocked this TowerModel Return all TowerToSimulations with this TowerModel Return all AbilityModel behaviors from this tower, if it has any Return the first ability on the tower Return a specific Ability of the tower. the TowerModel Index of the ability you want. Return all AttackModel behaviors for this TowerModel Return the first AttackModel from this TowerModel, if it has one Return the first AttackModel whose name contains the given string Return one of the AttackModels from this TowerModel. By default will give the first AttackModel The TowerModel Index of the AttackModel you want Recursively get every WeaponModels this TowerModel has Return one of the WeaponModels this TowerModel has. By default will return the first one The TowerModel Index of WeaponModel that you want Return the first WeaponModel this TowerModel has, if it has one. Sell every tower that uses this TowerModel Get the TowerId of this TowerModel. Equivalent to towerModel.name Duplicate this TowerModel with a unique name. Very useful for making custom TowerModels Set's the new towerId of this copy. By default the baseId will be set to this as well Applies a given ModDisplay to this TowerModel The type of ModDisplay Gets the ModTower associated with this TowerModel
If there is no associated ModTower, returns null Gets the specific ModTower associated with this TowerModel
If there is no associated ModTower, returns null Increase the range of a tower and all its attacks by the given amount Not Tested. Use to set the maximum allowed number of this tower Return all TowerDetailModels that share a base id with this towerModel Return the TowerPurchaseButton for this TowerModel. Return the number position of this TowerModel in the list of all tower models Return the current upgrade level of a specific path the TowerModel What tier of upgrade is currently applied to tower If this TowerModel is for a Hero, is this Hero unlocked? Has a specific upgrade for this TowerModel been unlocked already? the TowerModel Upgrade path Tier of upgrade Check if a specific upgrade path is being used/ has any upgrades applied to it the TowerModel Upgrade path to check Check if an upgrade has been applied Check if an upgrade has been applied Return all UpgradeModels that are currently applied to this TowerModel Return the UpgradeModel for a specific upgrade path/tier If this TowerModel is a Hero, return the HeroModel behavior Duplicate this TowerModel with a unique name. Very useful for making custom TowerModels Set's the new towerId of this copy. By default the baseId will be set to this as well Specify a new baseId. Set this if you want a baseId other than the newTowerId Check if this tower has speficif upgrade tiers Sets a TowerModel's tiers Format's the tower's name with its tiers Format's the tower's name with its tiers Gets the tower set (vanilla or modded) of this tower in the form of a string Gets whether a Tower/Hero is a base one added by the vanilla game. Extensions for UpgradeModels Return whether or not this upgrade has been unlocked by the player Gets the ModUpgrade associated with this UpgradeModel
If there is no associated ModUpgrade, returns null Gets the UpgradeModel that this UpgradePathModel uses Gets the ModUpgrade that this UpgradePathModel is for, or null if it's vanilla Extensions for WeaponModels Sets the emission for a WeaponModel, properly handling the child dependents Sets the emission for a WeaponModel, properly handling the child dependents Sets ejectX/ejectY/ejectZ all at once Sets ejectX/ejectY/ejectZ all at once Gets the eject position of the weapon as a vector Extensions for NK_TextMeshProUGuis Gets the localized text for the component Changes the text in the localization manager for this component Extensions for Bloons Return the DisplayNode for this bloon Return the UnityDisplayNode for this bloon. Is apart of DisplayNode. Needed to modify sprites Creates a new BloonToSimulation based off of this Bloon and stores it for possible later use. It will automatically destroyed when this Bloon is destroyed Return the Id of this Bloon Return the Factory that creates Bloons Return the existing BloonToSimulation for this specific Bloon. Tests whether a project will pop this bloon Returns whether or not the bloon was popped rather than leaked. True if it was popped, false if it was leaked or not destroyed yet Set bloon to be camo or not. Will change bloonModel to camo version if it exists the Bloon Should bloon be camo Set bloon to be fortified or not. Will change bloonModel to fortified version if it exists the Bloon Should bloon be fortified Set bloon to be regrow or not. Will change bloonModel to regrow version if it exists the Bloon Should bloon be regrow Remove current statuses from bloon the Bloon Should remove camo if present? Should remove fortify if present? Should remove regrow if present? Set the statuses of the bloon. Will change bloonModel if one exists with these statuses the Bloon Should have camo? Should have fortify? Should have regrow? Extensions for the BloonToSimulation Return the Id of this BloonToSimulation Return the DisplayNode for this bloon Return the UnityDisplayNode for this bloon. Is apart of DisplayNode. Needed to modify sprites Return the Simulation Bloon for this specific BloonToSimulation. Returns object of class Bloon Return the total distance this BloonToSim has travelled Extensions for Entities Get the DisplayNode for this Entity Get the UnityDisplayNode for this Entity Return the Factory that creates Entities Extensions for Maps Gets the Map's rectangle Extensions for Projectiles Get the DisplayNode for this Projectile Get the UnityDisplayNode for this Projectile. Will update display The Simulation Tower TowerModel to change to Return the DisplayNode for this Tower Return the UnityDisplayNode for this Tower. Is apart of DisplayNode. Needed to modify sprites Sell this tower Return the TowerToSimulation for this specific Tower Return the Factory that creates Towers Gets all other towers that are in range of this tower not including itself Get the MonkeyAnimationController for this Tower. Needed to modify 3D models Extensions for TowerToSimulation Return the Tower associated with this TowerToSimulation Extensions for StandardTowerPurchaseButtons Extension methods for System.Action Return this as a System.Action Invoke all actions in the list list of actions to invoke Invoke all actions in the list argument type list of actions to invoke argument to pass in while invoking Invoke all actions in the list list of actions to invoke Invoke all actions in the list argument type list of actions to invoke argument to pass in while invoking Invoke all actions in the list list of actions to invoke Invoke all actions in the list argument type list of actions to invoke argument to pass in while invoking Invoke all actions in the list list of actions to invoke Invoke all actions in the list argument type list of actions to invoke argument to pass in while invoking Extensions for Assemblies Gets the bytes for an embedded resource with the given name (found with endsWith), or null if no matches Extensions for normal System Dictionaries Get all of the values from this Dictionary as a list Extensions for DirectoryInfo Returns all Files in this directory that reference MelonLoader.dll or MelonLoader.ModHandler.dll Extensions for FileInfo Get all Assembly References from this FileInfo. Returns null if there are none Returns whether or not this File has a reference to the newer MelonLoader.dll or the older MelonLoader.ModHandler.dll Returns whether or not this File has a reference to the newer MelonLoader.dll (For MelonLoader 3.0 and up) Returns whether or not this File has a reference to the older MelonLoader.ModHandler.dll (For MelonLoader 2.7.4 and below) Extensions for streams Synchronously gets the full array of bytes from any stream, disposing with the Stream afterwards Extensions for strings Puts spaces between capitalized words within a string Returns null if a string is empty / whitespace, otherwise just returns back the string Gets the localization from the current localization text table, or the default one if it's not present in the current one. If the id is not present in any of these, returned as spaced or not spaced depending on parameters. The ID of the thing you're trying to get the localization of. Should this return the id with spaces if there's no localization present? Extensions for Type Get all methods with the specified method name Extensions for unity audio clips Plays a sound through the default Game AudioFactory The audio clip to play How loud it should be TODO group stuff Extensions for Buttons Adds an onclick function to a button Sets the onclick function of a button Removes the onclick function of a button Set the sprite for this button. Sprite to change to Optionally provide a new name for the sprite Extensions for Component Finds the component with the given path and type Try to get a component in a child of this Component by it's name. Equivelant to a foreach with GetComponentsInChildren Makes the Component visible by setting the scale to the default value of 1 Makes the Component hidden (not visible) by setting the scale to zero Translates this component scaled with it's "lossyScale", making it move the same amount regardless of screen resolution Destroys this Component Extensions for ButtonClickedEvents Adds a function to the click event Sets a function to be the only listener of a click event Remove a specific persistent call from a click event Removes all specific persistent call from a click event Extension for OnChangeEvent Adds a listener to a ValueChangedEvent Extensions for InputFieldSubmitEvents Adds a function to a submit event Extensions for SliderEvents Adds a listener to a slider event Extensions for ToggleEvents Add a function to a submit event Extensions for GameObjects Finds a component with the given path and type Try to get a component in a child of this GameObject by it's name. Equivelant to a foreach with GetComponentsInChildren Makes the Game Object visible by setting the scale to the default value of 1 Makes the Game Object hidden (not visible) by setting the scale to zero Destroys this GameObject Logs a GameObject's hierarchy recursively Translates this GameObject scaled with it's "lossyScale", making it move the same amount regardless of screen resolution Removes a Component from a GameObject by destroying it Returns whether a component of the given type exists on a game object Returns whether a component of the given type exists on a game object, and puts it in the out param Instantiate a clone of the GameObject keeping the same parent Instantiate a clone of the GameObject with the new transform as parent Adds the ModHelperComponent to a parent GameObject, returning the ModHelperComponent
(This is an extension method just so that we can return the type generically) Destroys all children of a game object Used to null check unity objects without bypassing the lifecycle Used to null check unity objects without bypassing the lifecycle Extensions for Images Set the sprite for this image Sprite to change image to Set the sprite for this image Sprite to change image to Loads a sprite reference to this image Sets the sprite of this image to one with the given name in the named sprite atlas Saves an image as a PNG files Coded in a robust manner that should work for all images, including those with multiple sprites on them being used Absolute file path on the machine to save the file to Set the sprite for this image Sprite to change image to Extensions for InputFields Add a submit event to an InputField \ Adds a value change event to an InputField Adds a value changed event to an InputField Extension methods for keycodes Gets the path string for the given key code Gets the Key code for a given path string Extensions for LayoutGroups Sets the padding on all sides of a panel to be a certain value Extension methods for the Localization Manager. Returns the text table that is currently in use. Extensions for PopupScreen Gets the InputField of the ActivePopup, or null Gets the NK_TextMeshProUGUI of the ActivePopup, or null Modifies the NK_TextMeshProUGUI of the most recently created popup Modifies the TMP InputField of the most recently created popup Since a recent BTD6 update, trying to show a popup while there already is one can cause a game crash. This method safely queues a popup for once there aren't any already active Extensions for unity renderers Set the texture for this renderer. Equivalent to "render.material.mainTexture = texture2D" Sets the outline color for this renderer Set the texture for all renderers in this collection. Equivalent to a "ForEach(render.material.mainTexture = texture2D)" Unbinds the renderer's sharedMesh, so that changes you make to it don't change the original Gets the list of triangles for a Mesh, even if its not marked as isReadable
Each "triangle" is a set of 3 consecutive ints in the list, where the number is the index in the vertices Gives this renderer the default outline for towers, also making them glow white when selected Extensions for Sprites Set this Sprite's texture Extensions for SpriteReferences Returns's the GUID of this SpriteReference Extensions for Texture2Ds Create Texture2D from a unity Color. Texture will only be this color Color to make new texture Save Texture2D as a png to file. File path to save texture to Attempts to save a Texture to a png at the given filePath, even if it isn't marked as readable Create Texture2D from a file on local PC path of file on PC Create a Sprite from this Texture2D Number of pixels you want in each unit. More pixels means bigger sprite in game Create a Sprite from this Texture2D Number of pixels you want in each unit. More pixels means bigger sprite in game Extensions for Toggles Adds a function to this toggle Extensions for UnityDisplayNodes Get all 3D models attached to this UnityDisplayNode. Gets the first generic renderer of the specified type, recalculating the renderers if need be The UnityDisplayNode Whether to recalculate renderers The type of Renderer you're looking for Gets all generic renderers on this UnityDisplayNode, recalculating the renderers if need be The UnityDisplayNode Whether to recalculate renderers Gets all generic renderers of the specified type, recalculating the renderers if need be The UnityDisplayNode Whether to recalculate renderers The type of Renderer you're looking for Gets the first (or an indexed) SkinnedMeshRenderer/MeshRenderer Gets all renderers that are of type SkinnedMeshRenderer or MeshRenderer Prints relevant info about this node to the console Removes (hides) a given bone Gets the transform associated with the given bone Saves the texture used for this node's mesh renderer
By default, this saves to local files, aka "C:\Users\...\AppData\LocalLow\Ninja Kiwi\BloonsTD6" The UnityDisplayNode Optional path to save to instead Dumps all textures for every renderer in the node Extensions for Vectors Convert UnityEngine.Vector3 to NinjaKiwi's SMath.Vector3 Catch-all class for non-extension static methods Directory where the Mod Helper stores most of its extra info Directory for where disabled mods are stored The directory path on the user's system that's set as their Mod Sources folder Gets whether this is running on net6 MelonLoader Gets whether the game is running as the Epic Store version Active mods that use ModHelper functionality All active mods, whether they're Mod Helper or not Gets a BloonsTD6Mod by its name, or returns null if none are loaded with that name
In this case a mod's name is its Assembly Name, which is almost always the same as the file name, but for the Mod Helper due to compatibility reasons it is "BloonsTD6 Mod Helper" rather than "Btd6ModHelper" Gets the instance of a specific BloonsTD6Mod by its type Returns whether a mod with the given name is installed Returns whether a mod with the given name is installed, and pass it to the out param if it is Logs a message from the specified Mod's LoggerInstance Logs a message from the specified Mod's LoggerInstance Logs an error from the specified Mod's LoggerInstance Logs a warning from the specified Mod's LoggerInstance Logs a message from the Mod Helper's LoggerInstance Logs a message from the Mod Helper's LoggerInstance Logs an error from the Mod Helper's LoggerInstance Logs a warning from the Mod Helper's LoggerInstance This is a pretty specific patch that addresses the following:
In the default game, the tower id specified in an UpgradePathModel is ONLY used in the UpgradeScreen for w/e reason. For actually upgrading the tower, it ignores it and just tries to find the next tower by its base id and tier. This patch changes it so that it actually respects what tower is being asked for in the UpgradePathModel.
This has no effect at all for vanilla towers, but allows modded towers to more easily do funky stuff with upgrades. NK never handled instances where a TowerCreateTower behavior was removed from the tower while it was still around. In this case, it ought to delete its created subtower. Prevent crashes for non-subtowers that aren't in the inventory Ignore any exceptions coming from this method This is a benign patch that helps fix unexpected issues with changing upgrade restrictions Stop the first object in the top upgrade row from being automatically selected, since it may not exist for modded towers Reserved to be used by the compiler for tracking metadata. This class should not be used by developers in source code. BloonsTD6 Mod Helper Provides Utility methods for dealing with BloonModels Constructs an accurate BloonID for a BloonModel based off of it's statuses. Creates a GrowModel behavior that adds Regrowth. Class for adding in a new Bloon to the game ModBloons with a BaseModBloon need to register after their base The ModBloon that this is based off of, or null if not based on a ModBloon The Bloon in the game that this should copy from as a base. Use BloonType.[Name] Set this to true if you're making another version of the BaseBloon, like a Fortified Red Bloon The Icon for the Bloon within the UI, by default looking for the same name as the file If you're not going to use a custom .png for your Icon, use this to directly control its SpriteReference Add the necessary properties to make this a Fortified Bloon Add the necessary properties to make this a Regrow Bloon The ID of the bloon that this should regrow into Whether this Bloon should use its Icon as its display The regrow rate Add the necessary properties to make this a Camo Bloon The list of displays to use as DamageStates for this Bloon For 2D bloons, the ratio between pixels and display units. Higher number -> smaller Bloon. For bloons with UseIconAsDisplay, the scale for the texture to use Apply your custom modifications to the base bloon Further modifies this bloon when you go into a new match. Useful for making conditional effects happen based on settings. The Base bloon model What GameModes are active for the match Class for a ModBloon which has a different ModBloon as its base The BaseBloon is the same as its base's Class for a custom RoundSet RoundSets register Bloons and before GameModes The Base Rounds included in the RoundSet specified by BaseRoundSet The id of the existing RoundSet to use as a base. Use RoundSetType.[name] If this RoundSetType.None, empty, or null, then an empty round set will be used The total number of rounds that have specified Bloons.
After this number of rounds, randomized Free Play rounds will happen The Icon for the Button for this RoundSet within the UI, by default looking for the same name as the file If you're not going to use a custom .png for your Icon, use this to directly control its SpriteReference Whether this Round set should show up in the menu allowing you to use any RoundSet for any GameMode Whether these rounds should have custom hints, like Alternate Bloons Rounds does Whether to bypass the base game hints settings. Useful for using your own ModSetting to control hints. Called to modify any/all rounds from 1 to Called to modify specifically just rounds from 1 to 40 Called to modify specifically just rounds from 41 to 60 Called to modify specifically just rounds from 61 to 80 Called to modify specifically just rounds from 81 to 100 Gets the custom hint for a specific round. This will be shown at the end of that round.
For no hint, return null. Modifies the GameModel that's used for matches played with this round set Allows you to easily modify the models of a specific vanilla Bloon The id of the vanilla Bloon to change Whether this should match by BaseId instead of Id.
If true, RedCamo would be affected as well if your was Red Gets the BloonModels affected by this ModVanillaBloon Allows you to easily modify the models of multiple vanilla Bloons The ids of the vanilla Bloon to change Whether this should match by BaseId instead of Id.
If true, RedCamo would be affected as well if your was Red Gets the BloonModels affected by this ModVanillaBloons Struct used to represent the name, position and size information of a ModHelperComponent The name of the ModHelperComponent's Unity GameObject The localPosition field, by default relative to the parent's center unless anchors are changed The localPosition x field, by default relative to the parent's center unless anchors are changed The localPosition y field, by default relative to the parent's center unless anchors are changed The sizeDelta field. This technically is how much the size should change compared to (anchorMax - anchorMin) Sets both the width and the height to be a certain value The sizeDelta x field. This technically is how much the width should change compared to (anchorMax.x - anchorMin.x) The sizeDelta y field. This technically is how much the width should change compared to (anchorMax.x - anchorMin.x) If this is part of a layout group, then the relative flexible width of this component. If this is part of a layout group, then the relative flexible height of this component. Sets both FlexWidth and FlexHeight to be a certain value The lower anchor. (0, 0) is the parent's lower left while (1,1) is the parent's upper right. Default (0.5, 0.5) Sets the X coordinate of the AnchorMin to be the specified value, leaving the Y coordinate unchanged (0.5) Sets the Y coordinate of the AnchorMin to be the specified value, leaving the X coordinate unchanged (0.5) The upper anchor. (0, 0) is the parent's lower left while (1,1) is the parent's upper right. Default (0.5, 0.5) Sets the X coordinate of the AnchorMax to be the specified value, leaving the Y coordinate unchanged (0.5) Sets the Y coordinate of the AnchorMax to be the specified value, leaving the X coordinate unchanged (0.5) Sets both AnchorMin and AnchorMax to the given value The local scale field to initialize width The point between (0, 0) and (1, 1) that this object rotates around and expands out from, by default (0.5, 0.5) The x component of the pivot The y component of the pivot Creates a new info struct representing the name, position and size of a ModHelperComponent The name of the ModHelperComponent's Unity GameObject The sizeDelta x field. This technically is how much the width should change compared to (anchorMax.x - anchorMin.x) The sizeDelta x field. This technically is how much the width should change compared to (anchorMax.x - anchorMin.x) Sets both the width and the height to be this value The localPosition x field, by default relative to the parent's center unless anchors are changed The localPosition y field, by default relative to the parent's center unless anchors are changed The localPosition x field, by default relative to the parent's center unless anchors are changed The localPosition y field, by default relative to the parent's center unless anchors are changed Sets both anchorX and anchorY to this value Sets both anchorX and anchorY to this value Sets both anchorX and anchorY to this value The point between (0, 0) and (1, 1) that this object rotates around and expands out from, by default (0.5, 0.5) Creates a new info struct representing the name, position and size of a ModHelperComponent The name of the ModHelperComponent's Unity GameObject A preset to apply Sets the properties of the RectTransform based on this Info object Creates a new Info with all the same properties as this Specific common preset setups of Info structs Will fill its parent component by matching its top left and bottom right with the parent's
Equivalent to { AnchorMin = new Vector2(0, 0), AnchorMax = new Vector2(1, 1) } Set alongside negative width/height to add padding around the edges, or positive to expand beyond Will fully flex horizontally and vertically,
Equivalent to { FlexWidth = 1, FlexHeight = 1 } Component to make this transform continuously match the position of another transform Other transform to constantly copy the scale from Offset from the transform to stay Scale for the original local position Component to track that a instance of a GameMenu's gameObject actually is the same ModGameMenu as was opened, as direct comparison on the Unity Objects does not work reliably The Id of the ModGameMenu that this is for ModHelperComponent for a background panel The aspect ratio of LongBtn sprites, since they aren't sliced for some reason lol The displayed image of the button The actual button component Creates a new ModHelperButton The name/position/size info The button's visuals What should happen when the button is clicked ModHelperComponent for a category in the mod settings menu Whether the category is hidden or not The panel that holds all the mod settings Creates a new ModHelperCategory The name of the category Whether it's collapsed by default or not The icon for the category, if any The created ModHelperCategory ModHelperComponent for a Checkbox The Toggle component The ModHelperImage for the Checkmark Whether it is currently checked or not Sets the current value of this The new value Whether the onValueChanged listener should fire Creates a new ModHelperCheckbox The name/position/size info If it starts out checked or not Action to perform when it is checked/unchecked, or null The background behind the check, or null for nothing The checkmark itself, or null for the default checkmark How much space around the outside of the check there is The new ModHelperCheckbox Base for a helper component for making custom UIs in the same style as Vanilla ones Default font size for UI labels Default alignment for texts and input fields Bool for if this should disable itself on the next frame Bool for if this should enable itself on the next frame The ModHelperComponent that this is a child of, if any The Info object that this was defined with, determining its initial name, position and size The RectTransform for this GameObject The LayoutElement component, if this has been assigned one The LayoutGroup component, if this has been assigned one Sets a particular transform to be the parent of this Sets a particular ModHelperComponent to be the parent of this Adds another ModHelperComponent as a child of this, returning the child Adds and returns a LayoutElement for this, making it work as part of a LayoutGroup Adds and returns a ContentSizeFitter with the given properties Gets a descendent component with the given name Sets whether or not this is active Applies the properties of an info struct to this Unity Component Update Deletes the underlying GameObject this is attached to, not just the component Implicitly get the gameObject Implicitly get the RectTransform Extensions for mod helper components, for using generics and based on restricts for il2cpp objects Adds the ModHelperComponent to a parent Transform, returning the ModHelperComponent
(This is an extension method just so that we can return the type generically) Adds the ModHelperComponent to a parent GameObject, returning the ModHelperComponent
(This is an extension method just so that we can return the type generically) Creates a copy of this ModHelperComponent with the same parent this Its new name ModHelperComponent for a The Image being displayed The Text which shows the currently selected value The component which handles the dropdown The Arrow image The template object for the window of the dropdown The default item in the template Creates a new ModHelperDropdown The name/position/size info. NOTE: width/height must be set to actual values The list of options Height of the created dropdown window Action that should happen when an option of the given index is selected The background image Text size of label The created ModHelperDropdown ModHelperComponent for a The Image being displayed Creates a new ModHelperImage The name/position/size info The sprite to display The created ModHelperImage Creates a new ModHelperImage The name/position/size info The sprite to display The created ModHelperImage ModHelperComponent for a text input field The InputField component The Text ModHelperComponent Gets the current value of the InputField Sets the current value of this The new text Whether the onValueChanged listener should fire Creates a new ModHelperInputField The name/position/size info The default text that's in the field The sprite for the background panel An action to be called when the text is changed The size of the displayed text The type of validation used on user input The alignment of the text The created ModHelperInputField ModHelperComponent that's the base panel for the visual representation of a ModSetting The displayed name for this setting The button that resets this setting The Icon for this setting. Will be null if no Icon provided The button that popups the description when pressed The top row of elements containing icon, name, info button The bottom row of elements containing the reset button and whatever input is added The image shown when this setting requires a restart Adds an action to the reset button Creates a new MoodHelperOption The displayed name of the mod setting The description of the mod setting, if any The icon of the mod setting, if any The created ModHelperOption ModHelperComponent for a background panel The background image Creates a new ModHelperPanel The name/position/size info The panel's background sprite If present, creates this panel with a Horizontal/Vertical layout group The layout group's spacing The layout group's padding The created ModHelperPanel ModHelperComponent for a background panel The ScrollContent object. This is the object that the children are actually part of, and is what actually moves up and down when scrolling. The ScrollRect component which controls many aspects of scrolling The Mask component which prevents overflow of rendering outside the scroll area The ContentSizeFitter component which makes sure that the ScrollContent is the right size to hold all the scrollable items. Adds a child to the ScrollContent of this panel Creates a new ModHelperScrollPanel The name/position/size info The axis that it scrolls in, or null for both directions The panel's background sprite If axis is not null, then the layout spacing for the items The created ModHelperScrollPanel ModHelperComponent for a sliding input The Default value, not scaled to anything The actual Slider component The panel that's the filled up bar part of the slider The real current value, scaled by the appropriate scale factor The image showing where the default value is on the slider The text that's on the notch of the slider Sets the current value of this The new value Whether the onValueChanged listener should fire Creates a new ModHelperSlider The name/position/size info. NOTE: height must be a set value The default slider amount The minimum value of the slider The maximum value of the slider What value the slider should increase by per tick The height and width of the pip Action should happen when the slider changes value, or null The size of the label text String to add to the end of the label, e.g. "%" If not null, the value that this should start as instead of the default ModHelperComponent for a background panel The component that handles the Text rendering Sets the text of this text to the given text Creates a new ModHelperText The name/position/size info AttackModels for making them easier to create This AttackModel's AttackFilterModel Default null Default empty Default 0 Default null Default includes the required FilterInvisibleModel Default null Default (0, 0, 0) Default false Default false Default 0 Default true Default 0 Default false Begins construction of a new AttackModel with sensible default values The model name (don't need the AttackModel_ part) Create an AttackAirUnitModel instead of an AttackModel Unwraps the model Wraps a model Helper for scaling costs to difficulties Gets a modified cost for a given set of ModModels that are used to setup a match Somewhere deep within those mods is likely to be a Cost modifier, and this will find and apply that The default cost The mods that the match is using The modified cost Gets a modified cost for a given GameModel's difficulty The default cost The current GameModel The modified cost Gets a modified cost for a given instance of InGame The default cost Current instance of InGame The modified cost Scales a base (medium) cost to the given difficulty Applies a multiplier to a cost and rounds it Class to help with the usage of Native File Dialogs Ensure that the NFD native dlls are present in the game root folder Class replacing the original functionality of FileIOUtil before BTD6 update 33.0 Same as the original FileIOUtil.sandboxRoot, INCLUDES A SLASH AT THE END Same as the original FileIOUtil.GetSandboxPath(), INCLUDES A SLASH AT THE END Saves an il2cpp object directly to the sandbox path like the original FileIOUtil.SaveObject
Will also create subdirectories as needed to save the file Name of file, extension included Same as the original FileIOUtil.LoadObject File name within the sandbox directory Same as the original FileIOUtil.SaveFile File name within the sandbox directory Text file contents to save Same as the original FileIOUtil.LoadFile File name within the sandbox directory Class for handily exporting elements of the GameModel to json files Exports every bit of GameModel and GameData info of note to the local folder Exports a Model to the path, returning whether it was successful. Does not log anything. Tries to save a specific Model and logs doing so Provides quick access to many major BTD6 singleton classes The instance of the Game class The instance of the GameData class The base GameModel class The current instance of the InGame class. Will be null if not in a match. The instance of the InGameData class that will be used for the next match The instance of the InGameData class for the current match The current UnityToSimulation bridge. Will be null if not in a game, or if the game isn't fully loaded in The current UnityToSimulation bridge. Will be null if not in a game, or if the game isn't fully loaded in The current game Simulation. Will be null if not in a game, or if the game isn't fully loaded in. The modified GameModel that's being used for the current game. Will be null if not in a game, or if the game isn't fully loaded yet The TowerManger for the current game. Will be null if not in a game, or if the game isn't fully loaded yet The InputManager for the current player. The CashManager for the current player. The current Btd6Player The current player's profile The current Display Factory The current FactoryFactory (factory for in game factories), or null if not in game The current Map in simulation, or null if not in game The current instance of the TowerSelectionMenu component, may be null The current instance of the PopupScreen component The current instance of the ShopMenu component, may be null The current NetworkedUnityToSimulation bridge. Will be null if not in a coop game, or if the game isn't fully loaded in The current NetworkedUnityToSimulation bridge. Will be null if not in a coop game, or if the game isn't fully loaded in The instance of the Btd6CoopGameNetworked class. Will be null if not in a coop game The instance of the GameConnection class. Will be null if not in a coop game The instance of the NKMultiConnection class. Will be null if not in a coop game The instance of the NKMultiGameInterface class. Will be null if not in a coop game Provides quick access to many major BTD6 object lists All towers currently placed in the game, or null if not in a game All TowerToSimulation objects currently placed in the game, or null if not in a game All Entities in the current game, or null if not in a game Contains helper methods for working with maps and custom maps. Create a out of an X and Y coord. Create a out of a Vector2. Create a out of X, Y, Z coords. Create a out of a Vector3. Creates a default PathModel out of list of Vector2 points Creates a default PathModel out of list of PointInfos Create a SpawnerModel based off of an array of paths A wrapper class around a Model The internal model Creates a wrapper around an existing model Helper methods for processes Exits the game and starts a new process after waiting 10 seconds, to ensure no "Another instance is already running" errors Opens a url in the default browser URL to open A wrapper around ProjectileModels for making them easier to create This ProjectileModel's ProjectileFilterModel This ProjectileModel's DisplayModel Default "" Default { guidRef = "" } Default .01 Default 0 Default 1 Default 0 (uncapped) Default the required ProjectileFilterModel and DisplayModel Default just the required FilterInvisibleModel Default false Default false Default false Default 1 Don't manually set these, they will be populated when the Helper is converted into a model Default false Default 0 Default 0 Default false Default false Default the name provided Default false Begins construction of a new ProjectileModel with sensible default values The model name (don't need the ProjectileModel_ part) Unwraps the model (and updates collision passes) Wraps a model A wrapper around WeaponModels for making them easier to create Default 1 Default 1 Default null Default (0, 0, 0) Default .2 Default false Default false Default SingleEmissionModel Default null Default false Default false Default 0 Default false Begins construction of a new WeaponModel with sensible default values The model name (don't need the WeaponModel_ part) Unwraps the model Wraps a model Utility functions used for sending messages over the network. Creates a message to be sent over the network. The message will be serialized as JSON. The object to be sent. The object's properties will be serialized. Unique code for your specific message. Reads a message sent from the network. Assumes message is sent as JSON. (via ) Raw bytes received from the network. Reads a message sent from the network. Assumes message is sent as JSON. (via ) Message received from the network. Old way to send a message Interface to signify that should be called on this type Class representing all the data ModHelper can utilize about a mod as separate from the MelonMod / .dll itself.
This is used for getting mod information from its GitHub repo, for getting information about enabled mods even if they don't want to have Mod Helper as a dependency, and keeping track of info about disabled mods. The ModHelperData objects for currently enabled mods ModHelperData for mods that are present in the Mods folder ModHelperData for mods that are in the disabled mods folder The currently active mod that this is associated with, if any Whether this mod is correctly in the Enabled mods folder Either a Mod's "Enabled" status is different from whether or not it's loaded into the game, or the data Version matches the repo's version and not the current version The place that the .dll file for this mod is on the local machine, if any Statically gets the Setters and Getters for easier accessing of the serialized fields A bunch of ModTextOverrides that all share the same Active condition and don't require any on the fly determinations of their text The table of keys and values Suffix added to all keys in the table, by default nothing "" Suffix added to all keys in the table, by default nothing "" Another class outside of the main BloonsTD6Mod class that ModSettings can be defined in. Rules otherwise work the same way Class for dynamically overriding In-Game text in a way that's compatible with other mods The key within the localization text table that this replaces Whether this is active or not at the given moment (that the text is retrieved) The text that will actually be returned if this is active The name of the asset bundle file that the model is in, not including the .bundle extension The name of the prefab that the model has within the Asset Bundle The name of the material that should be applied to the tower from the asset bundle, if any Whether to try loading the asset from the bundle asynchronously. Gets the base prototype from an asset bundle and given prefab name this mod to look for the asset bundle in completion action Finishes setting up the prototype, possible applying the material this the prototype game object the assetbundle in question completion action A ModCustomDisplay that will automatically apply to a ModBloon On a ModCustomDisplay, this property does nothing On a ModCustomDisplay, this property does nothing A ModDisplay that will automatically apply to a ModBloon The ModBloon that this ModDisplay is used for How damaged the Bloon is, higher numbers = use when more damaged Gets the display used by the given Bloon, optionally at a particular damage amount A convenient generic class for applying a ModBloonDisplay to a ModBloon Class for adding a new buff icon that can be displayed for towers The Icon for to display for the buff
(Name of .png or .jpg, not including file extension) If you're not going to use a custom .png for your Icon, use this to directly control its SpriteReference Whether the buff affects every tower on screen If greater than 0, the total number of stacks that a tower can have at one time Controls the OnlyShowBuffIfMutated property on the model What should go in a Model's buffLocsName field What should go in a Model's buffIconName field Makes a support model use this as its buff indicator The support model to apply to Makes a support model use this as its buff indicator The support model to apply to The custom version of a ModDisplay that loads in a model from a unity assetbundle On a ModCustomDisplay, this property does nothing Performs alterations to the unity display node when it is created A custom Display that is a copy of an existing Display that can be modified ModDisplays register first The GUID of the display to copy this ModDisplay off of The prefab reference itself of the base display that will be used The position offset to render the display at (z axis is up toward camera) The scale to render the display at How many pixels in a sprite texture should be equal to one unit If you modify the unity Object and not just the DisplayNode attached to it, then set this to true The DisplayCategory to use for the DisplayModel Alters the UnityDisplayNode that was copied from the one used by The prototype unity display node Allows you to modify this node asynchronously. On complete must be called for load to work! Takes place after the non-async ModifyDisplayNode call The prototype unity display node Callback for when you've finished changing the node Sets the mesh texture to that of a named png The UnityDisplayNode The name of the texture, without .png Sets the mesh texture to that of a named png The UnityDisplayNode The name of the texture, without .png The index to set at Sets the outline color for the first mesh renderer in the given node The UnityDisplayNode The color for it to be outlined (when not highlighted) Sets the outline color for the index'th mesh renderer in the given node The UnityDisplayNode The color for it to be outlined (when not highlighted) What index of mesh renderer to use Sets the sprite texture to that of a named png The UnityDisplayNode The name of the texture, without .png Gets a new DisplayModel based on this ModDisplay Gets the Display for a given tower, optionally for the given tiers The tower base id Path 1 tier Path 2 tier Path 3 tier The display GUID Gets the Display for a given bloon The bloon base id The display GUID Gets a UnityDisplayNode for a different guid The asset reference guid to get the node from What to do with the node Gets a UnityDisplayNode for a different guid The asset reference guid to get the node from What to do with the node Applies this ModDisplay to a given BloonModel Applies this ModDisplay to a given TowerModel Applies this ModDisplay to a given ProjectileModel Applies this ModDisplay to a given DisplayModel Applies this ModDisplay to a given EffectModel Applies this ModDisplay to a given EffectModel Gets the prototype to use for a callback in CreateAsync Recreated version of the CreateAsync_b__0 callback. Would like to just call this directly, but manually messing with the __DisplayClass_s proved buggy. The unity display node for the newly created display Creates and stores a new prototype for a prefab reference based on an original prototype Applies the effects of a ModDisplay on a UnityDisplayNode The display id for Road Spikes The display id for a Red Bloon Mod Display specifically set up to be a 2d image The file name (no .png) from your mod that you want to use as the 2d texture Class used internally to represent displays for s using and s using Creates a new Mod The mod this is for Id for the display to use Texture name, not a guid Scale to use A ModCustomDisplay that will automatically apply to a ModTower for specific tiers On a ModCustomDisplay, this property does nothing On a ModCustomDisplay, this property does nothing Performs alterations to the unity display node when it is created A convenient generic class for applying a ModTowerCustomDisplay to a ModTower A ModDisplay that will automatically apply to a ModTower for specific tiers Number of different Paragon displays that are used by default The ModTower that this ModDisplay is used for A number between 0 and 4 (inclusive) representing which set of paragon degrees this display applies to
0: Degree 1 - 20
1: Degree 21 - 40
2: Degree 41 - 60
3: Degree 61 - 80
4: Degree 81 - 100
If you don't have one for every index, then the next highest one will be used Returns true if this display should be used by its Tower for the given tiers The potential tiers of the tower If the Tower should have this display Applies this ModTowerDisplay to the towerModel. Override to change how this applies, i.e. making it apply to an AttackModel instead Alters the UnityDisplayNode that was copied from the one used by
By default, this will change the main texture of the first SkinnedMeshRenderer of the node to that of a png with the same name as the class The UnityDisplayNode If the tower tiers make it count as a Paragon A convenient generic class for applying a ModTowerDisplay to a ModTower In game IDs for the round sets included in BTD6 Controls how you want to wait the Task Scheduler to wait for your tasks Enum-like class for the different tower set types The primary set of towers The military set of towers The magic set of towers The support set of towers Enumeration of all (vanilla) tower sets Gets the ID to use for a custom tower set Further methods along the lines of Harmony's Safely gets the MethodInfo for a method within a nested class. This is recommended to use because over directly targeting it with typeof and nameof because the nested class names can change randomly. The outer type whose name won't change The name of nested type, not including the _s The desired method name within the nested type If multiple nested classes share a name portion, use the one at this index, default 0 The MethodInfo, or null alongside a console warning if one couldn't be found Class for scheduling Tasks using MelonCoroutines Schedule a task to execute right now as a Coroutine Schedule a task to execute later on as a Coroutine. By default will wait until the end of this current frame The action you want to execute once it's time to run your task Wait for this to be true before executing task Schedule a task to execute later on as a Coroutine The action you want to execute once it's time to run your task How you want to wait for your task The amount you want to wait /// Wait for this to be true before executing task Will wait for amountToWait before executing your Action. If a waitCondition is specified it will continue waiting amountToWait until waitCondition is true This coroutine will wait for amountToWait before finishing Dummy interface to unite ModContent and BloonsTD6Mod Initial task to register ModContent from other mods Don't load this like a normal task Wait for the bytes to all be loaded Static generic class that tracks Instances of ModContent Initial task to register ModContent from other mods Don't load this like a normal task Registers ModContent from other mods Task to preload images and save them in the Unity scene so they can be accessed quickly Don't load this like a normal task Handles the creation of an empty mod in the Mod Sources folder Creates an empty mod with the given name Generate the VanillaSprites.cs file
To get the necessary files, from the "...\BloonsTD6\BloonsTD6_Data\StreamingAssets\aa\StandaloneWindows64\Full\" folder, choose: asset_references_assets_all_[...].bundle initial_loading_ui_scenes_all_all_[...].bundle sprite_atlases_assets_all_[...].bundle ui_scenes_all_[...].bundle in Asset Studio. Then, select all assets of type Sprite, and in the menu do Export -> Dump -> Selected assets A class that can be generated by your mod in order to statically load a big complex Model object in your mod, like Ninja Kiwi does with the whole GameModel The array of object that NinjaKiwi programmed the loader to utilize Whether the Result has been Loaded yet The Task responsible for loading the bytes in the background The bytes that will be loaded from The file name of the bytes file within your project.
Remember to add it as an embedded resource! Set up the bytes when this is loaded Converts a generated Loader using normal System data structures to one that uses IL2Cpp ones Generates a ModByteLoader class and corresponding .bytes file within the BloonsTD6 directory The model that you want to be able to be statically loaded from bytes The absolute file path on your system where the .cs Loader file should end up The absolute file path on your system where the bytes file should end up If included, namespace to add to the loader cs file The NinjaKiwi generated method that loads the bytes Gets the result of the loader. Waits for loading to finish if need be. Perform additional memory clean up for the results ModContent serves two major purposes:
1. It is a base class for things that needs to be loaded via reflection from mods and given Ids, such as ModTower and ModUpgrade
2. It is a utility class with methods to access instances of those classes and other resources The BloonsTD6Mod that this content was added by The name that will be at the end of the ID for this ModContent, by default the class name The id that this ModContent will be given; a combination of the Mod's prefix and the name Backing property for ID that's only able to be overriden internally Used to allow some ModContent to Register before or after others The order that this ModContent will be loaded/registered in by Mod Helper. Useful for changing the ordering that it will appear in in-game relative to other content of this type in your mod, or for making certain content load before others like may be necessary for sub-towers. Default/0 will use arbitrary ordering. How many of this ModContent should it try to register in each frame. Higher numbers could lead to faster but choppier loading. Used for when you want to programmatically create multiple instances of a given ModContent Registers this ModContent into the game Creates the Instances of a ModContent type within a Mod and adds them to ModContentInstances Gets all loaded ModContent objects that are T or a subclass of T Gets all loaded ModContent objects that are exactly of type T Finds the loaded ModContent with the given Id and type T Gets the singleton instance of a particular ModContent or BloonsTD6Mod based on its type The type to get the instance of The singleton instance of it Gets the official instance of a particular ModContent or BloonsTD6Mod based on its type The type to get the instance of The official instance of it Gets a sprite reference by name for a specific mod The file name of your texture, without the extension Your mod's main BloonsTD6Mod extending class A new SpriteReference Gets a sprite reference by name for this mod The file name of your texture, without the extension A new SpriteReference Gets a sprite reference by name for a specific mod The BloonsTD6Mod that the texture is from The file name of your texture, without the extension A new SpriteReference Gets a sprite reference by name for a specific mod, returning null if the texture hasn't currently been loaded instead of an invalid SpriteReference The file name of your texture, without the extension Your mod's main BloonsTD6Mod extending class A new SpriteReference Gets a sprite reference by name for this mod, returning null if the texture hasn't currently been loaded instead of an invalid SpriteReference The file name of your texture, without the extension A new SpriteReference Gets a sprite reference by name for a specific mod, returning null if the texture hasn't currently been loaded instead of an invalid SpriteReference The BloonsTD6Mod that the texture is from The file name of your texture, without the extension A new SpriteReference Gets a sprite reference by name for a specific mod, or if the mod does not include a texture with that name, treats it as a vanilla sprite reference The BloonsTD6Mod that the texture is from The file name of your texture, without the extension A new SpriteReference Gets a sprite reference by name for a specific mod, or if the mod does not include a texture with that name, treats it as a vanilla sprite reference The file name of your texture, without the extension Your mod's main BloonsTD6Mod extending class A new SpriteReference Gets a sprite reference by name for a specific mod, or if the mod does not include a texture with that name, treats it as a vanilla sprite reference The file name of your texture, without the extension A new SpriteReference Returns a new SpriteReference that uses the given guid The guid that you'd like to assign to the SpriteReference Returns a new PrefabReference that uses the given guid The guid that you'd like to assign to the PrefabReference Returns a new AudioSourceReference that uses the given guid The guid that you'd like to assign to the AudioSourceReference Creates a Prefab Reference for a ModDisplay Creates a Sprite reference from the unsigned ints that can be found for a vanilla Sprite in
