name for a specific mod, to be used in SpriteReferences
Returns null if a Texture hasn't been loaded with that name The file name of your texture, without the extension Your mod's main BloonsTD6Mod extending class The texture's GUID Gets a texture's GUID by name for this mod
Returns null if a Texture hasn't been loaded with that name The file name of your texture, without the extension The texture's GUID Gets the id of a resource by appending the mod's ID prefix to its name Gets the id of a resource by appending the mod's ID prefix to its name Gets the id of a resource by appending the mod's ID prefix to its name Gets whether a texture with a given name has been loaded by the Mod Helper for a mod The mod to look in The file name of your texture, without the extension Gets whether a texture with a given name has been loaded by the Mod Helper for a mod The file name of your texture, without the extension The mod to look in Gets whether a texture with a given name has been loaded by the Mod Helper for this mod The file name of your texture, without the extension Constructs a Texture2D for a given texture name within a mod The mod that adds this texture The file name of your texture, without the extension A Texture2D Constructs a Texture2D for a given texture name within this mod The file name of your texture, without the extension A Texture2D Constructs a Texture2D for a given texture name within a mod The file name of your texture, without the extension A Texture2D Returns the Bytes associated with a texture. The file name of your texture, without the extension. The bytes associated with the texture. Returns the Bytes associated with a texture. The mod that adds this texture. The file name of your texture, without the extension. The bytes associated with the texture. Returns the Bytes associated with a texture. The file name of your texture, without the extension. The bytes associated with the texture. Constructs a Sprite for a given texture name within a given mod The file name of your texture, without the extension The pixels per unit for the Sprite to have A Sprite Constructs a Sprite for a given texture name within a given mod The file name of your texture, without the extension The pixels per unit for the Sprite to have A Sprite Constructs a Sprite for a given texture name within this mod The file name of your texture, without the extension The pixels per unit for the Sprite to have A Sprite Gets a bundle from a mod with the specified name (no file extension) Gets a bundle from the mod T with the specified name (no file extension) Gets a bundle from your mod with the specified name (no file extension) Gets a BloonsTD6Mod by its name, or returns null if none are loaded with that name Returns whether a mod with the given name is installed Returns whether a mod with the given name is installed, and pass it to the out param if it is Gets the ID for the given ModBloon Gets the GUID (thing that should be used in the display field for things) for a specific ModDisplay Gets an AudioClip from a mod by its name (no file extension included) The mod Sound name (no .wav) a playable AudioClip Gets an AudioClip from a mod by its name (no file extension included) Sound name (no .wav) The mod a playable AudioClip Gets an AudioClip from this mod by its name (no file extension included) Sound name (no .wav) a playable AudioClip Gets an AudioSource reference for a given sound within a mod Sound name (no .wav) An AudioSoundReference Gets an AudioSource reference for a given sound within a mod Sound name (no .wav) The mod An AudioSoundReference Gets an AudioSource reference for a given sound within a mod Sound name (no .wav) The mod An AudioSoundReference Gets an AudioSource reference for a given sound within this mod Sound name (no .wav) An AudioSoundReference Gets the ID for the given ModGameMode Gets the ID for the given ModRoundSet Gets the internal tower name/id for a ModTower The top path tier The middle path tier The bottom path tier The ModTower type The tower name/id Gets the TowerModel for a ModTower at a specific tier level The top path tier The middle path tier The bottom path tier The ModTower type The tower name/id Gets the internal upgrade name/id for a ModUpgrade The ModUpgrade type The upgrade name/id Gets the fabricated TowerSet enum value for a ModTowerSet Http client used by the mod helper The HttpClient instance Initializes the HttpClient Asynchronously downloads from a url to the given file path, returning whether the operation was successful URL to download from File path for the resulting file Whether it was sucessful Downloads a zip file directly into a zip archive Downloads and extracts the contents of a zip file into the Zip Temp directory, returning the file paths of the extracted files URL to download from Path to unzip into, or null for using the zip temp directory Enumeration of extracted file paths, or null Class for a Coroutine style task that runs during the BTD6 loading screen Whether to show the progress bar during this task or not If is enabled, how much Progress should be shown (from 0 to 1) The subtext that appears to the right of the Display Name at the bottom of the loading screen
Can be dynamically changed while running the task Coroutine style function Class for keeping track of a variable for a Mod that can be changed in game via the Mod Settings menu The type that this ModSetting holds Will only save the result and run onSave if this custom function validates the value Action to call when the value is saved, i.e. once they actually close the menu Action to call when the value changes within the menu Constructs a new ModSetting for the given value Old onValueChanged. Base class for a ModSetting without the generics The category that this is part of, or null The description / explanation of this mod setting The exact name displayed for this mod setting. If unset, will use the variable name. Icon to display alongside the setting Action to modify the ModHelperOption after it's created Indicates to players that the effects of changing this setting will only take place after a restart The type where this ModSettings was defined Gets the current value that this ModSetting holds The value Gets the default value for this ModSetting The default value Sets the current value of this ModSetting The new value Constructs a visual ModHelperComponent for this ModSetting The created ModHelperComponent Validates the current value using the customValidation function, if there is one. If there were no issues, performs the onSave action Creates a base ModHelperOption component based on the name, description and icon of this ModSetting class for a boolean value Whether this should display as an Enabled/Disabled button instead of a checkbox The sprite to use for the button when it's disabled, RedBtnLong by default The text that the button should have when it's disabled, if this is a button The sprite to use for the button when it's enabled, GreenBtnLong by default The text that the button should have when it's enabled, if this is a button Action to modify the ModHelperCheckbox after it's created Action to modify the ModHelperCheckbox after it's created Old way to do a button before ModSettingButton was a thing Create a new ModSetting bool with the given value as default Gets the current value out of a ModSettingBool ModSetting for adding a button in the settings that performs a given action, with the setting just tracking the number of times that the button has been pressed. The action that this button performs The sprite to use for the button, BlueBtnLong by default The text that will appear on the button, "Dew It" by default Action to modify the ModHelperButton after it's created Category of mod settings Whether this category is currently collapsed / hiding its elements Name of the category Icon of the category, if any Action to modify the ModHelperCategory after it's created Order of this category in relation to other categories. A setting not having a category will have order = 0 Creates a new ModSettingCategory with the given displayName Creates the visual ModHelperCategory for this option Gets the name from a category Creates a new category with the given name ModSetting for a decimal value Old way of doing slider Old way of doing max Old way of doing min Step size to use for slider, or how much to round the input Constructs a new ModSetting with the given value as default Constructs a new ModSetting with the given value as default Gets the current value out of a ModSetting Gets the current value out of a ModSetting ModSetting for an Enum value The Enum in question A function to apply to each enum value to get the label for it Action to modify the ModHelperDropdown after it's created Constructs a new ModSetting with the given value as default Gets the current value out of a ModSetting ModSetting for selecting a specific file on the host computer https://github.com/mlabbe/nativefiledialog/blob/master/README.md#file-filter-syntax Constructs a new ModSetting with the given value as default Gets the current value out of a ModSetting Alias for ModSetting for selecting a specific folder on the host computer Constructs a new ModSetting with the given value as default Gets the current value out of a ModSetting ModSetting for a customizable Hotkey Returns whether the Hotkey was pressed down on this frame Returns whether the Hotkey is currently being pressed / held Returns whether the Hotkey just went from being pressed to not being pressed on this frame Creates a new ModSettingHotkey from a KeyCode ModSetting for int values Old way of doing slider Old way of doing max Old way of doing min Constructs a new ModSetting with the given value as default Gets the current value out of a ModSetting ModSetting class for a number, implying it can have a min/max value, and be an input or a slider The largest allowed value, or null for unbounded The lowest allowed value, or null for unbounded Action to modify the ModHelperInputField after it's created Action to modify the ModHelperSlider after it's created Whether this displays as a slider Suffix to add to the end of the slider label Step Size for the slider Validation to use for the input component Turn the value into a string for labels Get the value from the string input component Conversion of the type to a float for the slider component Conversion of the type from a float for the slider component ModSetting for a string value Allow all characters Allow only alphanumeric characters Allow only valid decimals Allow only valid integers Validation for the input field, determining which characters are allowed Action to modify the ModHelperInputField after it's created InputField validation, use one of the ModSettingString.[thing] constants Constructs a new ModSetting with the given value as default Gets the current value out of a ModSetting ModContent with DisplayName and Description that registers values in the LocalizationManger's textTable The name that will be actually displayed for this in game The name that will actually be display when referring to multiple of these The in game description of this Registers the text for this in the LocalizationManager Class for a custom GameMode that will be added to the modes screen when starting a new match Registers after Round Sets Where this Mode should show up within the Mode Select screen. Use DifficultyType.[name] The id of the existing GameMode to use as a base. Use GameModeType.[name] If this GameModeType.None, empty, or null, then an empty base will be used Whether this GameMode ... The Icon for the Button for this Mode within the UI, by default looking for the same name as the file If you're not going to use a custom .png for your Icon, use this to directly control its SpriteReference Implemented by a ModGameMode to modify the base game mode, for instance by adding or removing mutator mods Modifies the GameModel that's used for matches played with this mode Class for adding a custom Hero to the game. Use alongside to give multiple levels. Heroes aren't in the default shop No paragon heroes The default hero (or tower) to base your hero off of Heroes can only be in the Hero tower set Putting all the hero level upgrades in the top path No other upgrade paths used No other upgrade paths used The other hero that has the same colored name in the Heroes menu as you want to use The other hero that has the same glow color in the Heroes menu as you want to use The other hero that has the same background color in the Heroes menu as you want to use The png name of the Button icon for this hero in the UI, by default Name-Button The exact sprite reference used for the button The name of the png to try to find for the new hero select screen button The SpriteReference for this hero's Square icon in the new revamped HeroScreen If you want to manually override which portraits your hero uses in the select screen, mess with this
By default will find any s defined in your s
The SpriteReference is the actual image that will be displayed The total number of levels this hero has. Do not set this to anything other than number of ModHeroLevels that you've actually created for your Hero. XpRatio to use when determining the default xp costs of the levels.
All four base heroes (Quincy, Gwendolin, Striker Jones, Obyn Greenfoot) as well as Etienne have an XP ratio of 1x.
Ezili, Pat Fusty, Admiral Brickell, and Sauda have a 1.425x XP ratio.
Benjamin and Psi have an XP ratio of 1.5x.
Captain Churchill and Adora have a ratio of 1.71x. The short description that appears under the name of the hero The description to use for the first level of your hero The total number of abilities that this hero has as max level
OBSOLETE: No longer required to manually specify Heroes tower tiers are always Level-0-0 Gets the font material for the default SkinData Existing hero skins by their skin/tower name Gets the Background Banner for the default SkinData Existing hero skins by their skin/tower name Gets the background color for the default SkinData Existing hero skins by their skin/tower name Creates the SkinData for the default tower Existing hero skins by their skin/tower name Sound to play when you select this hero in the hero select screen, the sound must be registered in the game for it to play The index to add this hero at in relation to other heroes Class representing the UpgradeModel and changes for a particular Level for a ModHero Internal naming scheme for hero levels No confirmation on hero upgrades No confirmation on hero upgrades No confirmation on hero upgrades Hero upgrades don't have individual icons Hero upgrades don't have individual icons The upgrade's tier is the hero's level. Hero upgrades have no cost How much XP the hero needs to get to go from the previous level to this level.
Default is calculated the same way Ninja Kiwi does it using The ModTower is the ModHero All hero upgrades count as top path What level this The tower that this is an upgrade for DisplayName field of the AbilityModel added at this level, if any Description of the ability added at this level, if any The filename without extension for the portrait this Level should make the hero start using
By default, the of the with the appended, e.g. "IndustrialFarmer-Portrait3" Convenient generic class for specifying the ModHero that this ModHeroLevel is for Defines the Paragon Upgrade for a ModTower. Remember to set the property. Specifically use the paragon upgrade naming scheme. No overriding because that apparently causes issues. Override the ID to not have the prefix. It's necessary to work, and there's not a good way for different paragon mods to coexist anyway No changing of ModParagonUpgrade path No changing of ModParagonUpgrade tier The ParagonTowerModel that this will use as a base. You don't need to worry about displayDegreePaths By default, remove any abilities from the Paragon tower No loading of multiple ModParagonUpgrades Modify the PowerDegreeMutator of the Paragon Method to modify the Simulation Tower once its Degree has been set A convenient generic class for specifying the ModTower that this ModParagonUpgrade is for Helper class for making a subtower Helper class for making a subtower for a specific other ModTower Class for adding a custom Tower to the game. Use alongside to define its upgrades, and optionally to define custom displays for it. The string to use for the Primary tower set The string to use for the Magic tower set The string to use for the Military tower set The string to use for the Support tower set Constructor for ModTower, used implicitly by ModContent.Create ModTowers register third The Portrait for the 0-0-0 tower, by default "[Name]-Portrait"
(Name of .png or .jpg, not including file extension) The Icon for the Tower's purchase button, by default "[Name]-Icon"
(Name of .png or .jpg, not including file extension) If you're not going to use a custom .png for your Icon, use this to directly control its SpriteReference If you're not going to use a custom .png for your Portrait, use this to directly control its SpriteReference Whether this Tower should display 2-dimensionally, and search for png images
For 2D towers, the ratio between pixels and display units. Higher number -> smaller tower. Makes this Tower not actually add itself to the shop, useful for making subtowers The family of Monkeys that your Tower should be put in.
For now, just use one of the default constants provided of PRIMARY, MILITARY, MAGIC, or SUPPORT. The id of the default BTD Tower that your Tower is going to be copied from by default. The in game cost of this tower (on Medium difficulty) The number of upgrades the tower has in it's 1st / top path The number of upgrades the tower has in it's 2nd / middle path The number of upgrades the tower has in it's 3rd / bottom path Whether to always make this tower be included in challenges / Bosses / Odysseys etc How many of this tower you can buy at once during a game. By default -1 for no limit. Defines whether / how this ModTower has a Paragon Hotkey to use for placing this tower from the shop Implemented by a ModTower to modify the base tower model before applying any/all ModUpgrades
Things like the TowerModel's name, cost, tier, and upgrades are already taken care of before this point The Base Tower Model Further modifies this tower when you go into a new match. Useful for making conditional effects happen based on settings.
The normal ApplyUpgrade effects for all upgrades will have already been applied on game start, so this will simply modify all the TowerModels for this ModTower. Returns all the valid tiers for the TowerModels of this Tower Another way to modify the allowable crosspaths for your tower. By default, checks that the highest tier is at most 5, the next highest is at most 2, and the last one is 0
Used in the default implementation of If this is a 2D tower, gets the name of the .png to use for a given set of tiers
Default Behavior Example: For CardMonkey with tiers 2-3-0, it would try (in order): CardMonkey-230, CardMonkey-X3X, CardMonkey-2XX, CardMonkey Gets the scale to use for a 2d tower at the given tiers Gets the portrait reference this tower should use for the given tiers
Looks for the highest tier this tower has that defined a , falling back to the tower's own base by default. Gets the base TowerModel for this Tower to use at the given tiers The 0-0-0 TowerModel for this Tower When adding this tower to the shop, gets the index at which to add the tower relative to the existing ones.
By default, the tower will be put at the end of the TowerSet category that it's in. Creates an UpgradePathModel for a particular upgrade and new tiers The upgrade The new desired tiers of the tower Allows you to override how many possible Upgrade pips it should show as being possible for a tower to get The TowerToSimulation What path this is for The default maximum The new maximum amount of upgrade pips, or null for no change Allows you to override whether an UpgradePath should be closed or not for a tower The TowerToSimulation What path this is for Whether it'd be naturally closed or not Whether the upgrade path should be closed, or null for no change Allows you to change the base TowerModel your tower will use at different tiers. Note that you'd need to be careful if you entirely changed the base tower you're working with at different tiers, as it will still attempt to apply all the appropriate upgrades. If you would like a ModUpgrade to only have an effect at a given tier, you could do something like: public override void ApplyUpgrade(TowerModel towerModel) { if (towerModel.tiers[Path] != Tier) return; ... } Length 3 array of Top/Mid/Bot tiers The base TowerModel to use A convenient generic class for specifying the ModTowerSet that a ModTower uses The custom tower set that this ModTower uses Defines the Paragon behavior for a ModTower Don't generate a Paragon Generate a Paragon by applying the ModParagonUpgrade to the 000 version of the tower Generate a Paragon by applying the ModParagonUpgrade to the 555 version of the tower Class with helper methods for TowerModels / ModTowers
Mostly used internally Creates and returns an empty TowerModel A custom collection of ModTowers Internal int enum value used for this ModdedTowerSet TowerSet enum for this modded TowerSet ModTowerSets register before ModTowers, alongside ModUpgrades Unused Unused Name of .png file for the background for towers in the Monkeys menu and the in game shop SpriteReference for the container Name of .png file for the background used for non-paragon upgrades in the Upgrade screen SpriteReference for the large container Name of .png file for the group button used in the Monkeys menu SpriteReference for the button Name of .png file for the background for in game portraits SpriteReference for the portrait Name of .png file for the seat to use in Odyssey mode (theoretically) SpriteReference for the Seat Whether this Tower Set should still be allowed to appear in Primary Only, Military Only, Magic Only Where to place this ModTowerSet in relation to other towerSets. By default at the end.
The current towerSets that already exist No loading multiple instances of a ModTowerSet The position to start placing ModTowers of this ModTowerSet in relation to other towers
By default, will determine the position based on GetTowerSetIndex
The set of all current tower details A class used to create an Upgrade for a Tower Path ID for the Top path Path ID for the Middle path Path ID for the Bottom path Path ID for the Top path Path ID for the Middle path Path ID for the Bottom path ModUpgrades register second The file name without extension for the Portrait for this upgrade
By default is the same file name as the tower followed by -Portrait The file name without extension for the Icon for this upgrade
The Tower follows the default Bloons method of picking a Portrait: choose the highest tier upgrade, and if there's a tie, choose Mid > Top > Bot (for whatever reason)
By default is the same file name as the tower followed by -Icon If you're not going to use a custom .png for your Icon, use this to directly control its SpriteReference If you're not going to use a custom .png for your Portrait, use this to directly control its SpriteReference Custom priority to make this upgrade applied sooner (increased priority) or later (decreased priority) when the TowerModel is being constructed Whether this upgrade requires a confirmation popup The title for the confirmation popup, if needed The body text for the confirmation popup, if needed Xp Cost for the upgrade. Meaningless usually because custom heroes automatically are automatically unlocked. The upgrade path Use , , The upgrade tier, 1 for Tier 1 Upgrades, 2 for Tier 2, etc... How much the upgrade costs on Medium difficulty The tower that this is an upgrade for Assigns this upgrade to its ModTower Apply the effects that this upgrade has onto a TowerModel
The TowerModel's tier(s), applied upgrades and other info will already be correct, so this is mostly about changing the TowerModel's behavior
The default ordering of upgrade application is to do them in ascending order of tier, doing Top then Mid then Bot at each tier. This can be changed using . The Tower Model Make this upgrade apply additional effects on a towerModel when you go into a new match. Useful for making conditional effects happen based on settings.
The normal ApplyUpgrade effects for all upgrades will have already been applied on game start, so this will simply modify all the TowerModels for this ModTower that have this upgrade. Apply effects to this Tower Model before all other ApplyUpgrade and LateApplyUpgrade effects have happened
Otherwise, usual priority / ordering rules still apply Apply effects to this Tower Model after all the other EarlyApplyUpgrade and ApplyUpgrade effects have happened
Otherwise, usual priority / ordering rules still apply If you really need to override the way that the ModUpgrade makes its UpgradeModel, go ahead Allows you to dynamically allow an upgrade to not be purchasable based on the InGame values of a Tower If A convenient generic class for specifying the ModTower that this ModUpgrade is for Class for changing Vanilla content within the game Whether this should only modify the Towers In-Game, or also affect the default GameModel outside a game Change the name of it Change the description of it Whether this should apply or not. Useful for ModSettings Gets the TowerModels that this will affect in the GameModel Applies the modifications to the vanilla content Applies the modifications to the vanilla content ModContent Class for modifying a certain set of vanilla towers Gets the TowerModels that this will affect in the GameModel Applies the modifications to the vanilla content Applies the modifications to the vanilla content Dummy ModTower that can be used to make a Paragon for a base tower. No paragons in shop Not using the custom tower paragon mode Order doesn't apply here Same towerSet as base tower Handled by upgrade cost No upgrades for the paragon No upgrades for the paragon No upgrades for the paragon Controlled by the ModParagonUpgrade Controlled by the ModParagonUpgrade Controlled by the ModParagonUpgrade Controlled by the ModParagonUpgrade Controlled by the ModParagonUpgrade Name override Tower gets modified in the Paragon upgrade Doesn't generate any of the tower on its own Tower index doesn't apply ModContent class for modifying all TowerModels for a given Tower The base id of the Tower that this should modify all TowerModels of
Use TowerType.[tower] Change the name of it when it's plural Change the TowerSet that this tower is part of. Also handles moving its place within the shop. Changes the base cost Helper class for changing a vanilla tower to be part of a modded tower set ModContent class for modifying all TowerModels that have a given upgrade applied to them Changes the base cost The id of the Upgrade that this should modify all TowerModels that use
Use UpgradeType.[upgrade] Change the UpgradeModel for this upgrade Class to statically store RuntimeAnimationControllers for different animations Gets an AnimationController by its name, or null if there isn't one with that name Class to statically store TMP_FontAsset for different fonts Gets an AnimationController by its name, or null if there isn't one with that name Class for a custom BTD6 menu The text of the Header component that's on many UI screens, might be null Whether the menu is currently closing The string name of the in game menu to copy from Whether this Menu is open or not The current GameMenu Runs right as your custom menu is being opened, with the optional data argument that can be passed into Whether to run the base menu's OnOpen code Runs right as your custom menu is being closed Runs every time that your custom menu updates The name NinjaKiwi gave to the menu of the given screen type Opens a custom menu The custom data to pass into your ModGameMenu's method The data that you want to pass into the base menu's Open method, if you're still running the code The custom menu type to open Generic class for creating a ModGameMenu with the given type as it's base menu Class to be passed in to the Open methods of Screens The data that the base menu receives, if the Open code is still run The id of the ModGameMenu this is for The data that the ModGameMenu receives Creates a ModMenuData object with the given Id and data This class is used in the API to store data about the current state of the game, like whether or not the player is in a Public Coop game Contains all the Bloons that were leaked during this round Used to track which bloons were popped and which leaked Contains all the Bloons that were destroyed during this round If the player is in Coop, this value represents whether it's a Public Coop match or not If the player is in a game, are they in a Odyssey game If the player is in a game, is it a Race game The directory of the save file. The instance of that is used during a multiplayer game If the player is in a game, is it currently paused How much cash each bloon is worth when completely popped Singleton instance of SessionData Keeping track of popped bloons Resets all the values in SessionData Class to access the game's Main Menu UI Gets the Scene for the Main Menu Gets the Canvas for the Main Menu Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Heroes Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Play Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Gets the Monkeys Button if on the Main Menu, or null Class to access the game's Map Select UI Gets the Scene if on the Map Select menu Gets the Canvas for the MapSelectUI, or null Gets the Beginner Button if on the Map Select Menu, or null Gets the Intermediate Button if on the Map Select Menu, or null Gets the Advanced Button if on the Map Select Menu, or null Gets the Expert Button if on the Map Select Menu, or null Gets the Extreme Button if on the Map Select Menu, or null Class to access the Title Screen UI Gets the Scene if on the Title Screen menu = Gets the Canvas for the TitleScreenUI, or null Gets the Intermediate Button if on the Map Select Menu, or null Block clicks to objects above the webview Don't draw the SteamWebView on the entire screen when using the RawImage Keep the CurrentUrl up to date Allow mod files to be downloaded through the browser Use a RawImage to render if available so that objects can be displayed on top of it The ModGameMenu for the screen showing current mods Update the currentTowerSet tracker, and change the state if need be Changes the order of the TowerDetails in the GameModel
Their order in the GameModel is what determines their order in the screen Put the GameModel's TowerDetails ordering back to normal Change the MonkeySelectButtons on the current page Actually make the displayed buttons change Possible inputs:
towerSet=null swipeGesture=false (reOpening=false) - When opening the MonkeySelectMenu from the Main Menu
towerSet=not null swipeGesture=true (reOpening=false) - When swiping or clicking the left/right buttons
towerSet=not null swipeGesture=false (reOpening=false) - When clicking the MonkeyGroupButtons, also the initial call to Open()
towerSet=not null swipeGesture=false (reOpening=true) - Called during our calls to Open() to change the buttons Class controlling the in game Round Set override UI The round set override currently chosen, or null Expanded version of MelonMod to suit the needs of BTD Mod Helper Lets the ModHelper control patching, allowing for individual patches to fail without the entire mod getting unloaded. All ModContent in ths mod The settings in this mod organized by name The embedded resources (textures) of this mod Audio clips for the embedded sounds in this mod The prefix used for the IDs of towers, upgrades, etc for this mod to prevent conflicts with other mods Setting this to true will prevent your BloonsTD6Mod hooks from executing if the player could get flagged for using mods at that time.
For example, using mods in public co-op The path that this mod would most likely be at in the Mod Sources folder Signifies that the game shouldn't crash / the mod shouldn't stop loading if one of its patches fails Allows you to define ways for other mods to interact with this mod. Other mods could do: ModHelper.GetMod("YourModName")?.Call("YourOperationName", ...); to execute functionality here.
A string for the name of the operation that another mods wants to call The parameters that another mod has provided A possible result of this call Manually adds new ModContent to the mod. Does not directly call or , but the latter will still end up being called if this is added before the Registration phase. Manually adds multiple new ModContent to the mod. Does not directly call or , but the latter will still end up being called if this is added before the Registration phase. Tries to apply all Harmony Patches defined within a type. Logs a warning and adds a load error if any fail. Called whenever the Mod Options Menu gets opened, after it finishes initializing Called when the settings for your mod are saved. The json representation of the settings about to be saved Called when the settings for your mod are loaded The json representation of the settings that were just loaded Extend this Class instead of MelonMod to gain access to dozens of easy to use built-in hooks Executed once the user has connected to a game session. Note: Only invoked if == true. The interface used to interact with the game. Executed once the user has tried to connect to a server, but failed to do so. Note: Only invoked if == true. The interface used to interact with the game. Executed once the player has disconnected from a server. Note: Only invoked if == true. The interface used to interact with the game. Executed when a new client has joined the game session. Note: Only invoked if == true. The interface used to interact with the game. Index of the peer in question. Executed when a new client has left the game session. Note: Only invoked if == true. The interface used to interact with the game. Index of the peer in question. Acts on a Network message that's been sent to the client
Use to read back the message you sent.
If this is one of your messages and you're consuming and acting on it, return true. Otherwise, return false. Seriously. Note: Only invoked if == true. Called when a map model is loaded by the game. Equivelant to MapLoader.Load. The map that was just loaded. It is passed by reference to allow for modifications. Called when the player's ProfileModel is loaded.
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on ProfileModel.Validate Perform actions on a profile right before the Mod Helper cleans it. If you see that the Mod Helper cleans past profile data from your mod on startup, it should be removed here. If you removed any data in PreCleanProfile, you may want to add it back here, or just call again on the profile. Called when InGame.instance.UnityToSimulation.Simulation is not null Called when Game.instance.model is not null Called when a new GameModel is created, aka when things like Monkey Knowledge are applied to towers
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on GameModel.CreatedModded Called when the TowerInventory is initialized for a game Called when a new GameModel is created, aka when things like Monkey Knowledge are applied to towers
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on GameModel.CreatedModded Called when a new GameModel is created, aka when things like Monkey Knowledge are applied to towers
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on GameModel.CreatedModded Called when a new GameModel is created, including the map
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on GameModel.CreatedModded Called when a display is being loaded such as a towers 3d model
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on GameModel.CreatedModded Called when the games audioFactory is loaded
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on AudioFactory.Start Called when a sprite is being loaded
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on ResourceLoader.LoadSpriteFromSpriteReferenceAsync Called when the GameData.Instance object is first loaded GameData.Instance Called before a sprite is loaded from a SpriteAtlas Return 'false' to prevent the original method from running
Equivalent to a HarmonyPrefix on SpriteAtlas.GetSprite Called after a sprite is loaded from a SpriteAtlas
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on SpriteAtlas.GetSprite Called when you go to the main menu screen
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on MainMenu.OnEnable Called right after a match ends in victory
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on InGame.OnVictory Called right after a match is started up (restart included it seems like)
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on InGame.StartMatch Called when a match is restarted
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on InGame.Restart Called right after a game ends in victory
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on TimeManager.SetFastForward Called right after the game finishes loading everything
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on TitleScreen.Start Called when the player returns to the MainMenu from a match
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on InGame.Quit Called when the pause screen is opened
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on PauseScreen.Open Called when the pause screen is closed
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on PauseScreen.Close Called right before a Bloon would leak. Return 'false' to prevent the leak from happening
Equivalent to a HarmonyPreFix on Bloon.Leaked Called right after a Bloon leaks.
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Bloon.Leaked Called right after a Bloon is first created
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Bloon.Initialise Called right after a Bloon's BloonModel is updated
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Bloon.UpdatedModel Called right after a Bloon is destroyed
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Bloon.OnDestroy Called right after a Bloon is destroyed, but only when it's popped and not leaked Called right after a Bloon is damaged
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Bloon.Damaged Called after a new bloon emission is added to the spawner
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on Spawner.AddEmissions Called after a bloon is emitted from a spawner
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on Spawner.Emit Called right before a Tower's 3D graphics are initialized
Equivalent to a HarmonyPreFix on InputManager.CreateTowerGraphicsAsync Called right after a Tower is initialized
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Tower.Initialise Called right after a Tower is destroyed
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Tower.Destroyed Called right after a Tower is sold
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Tower.OnSold Called right after a Tower is selected by the player
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on TowerSelectionMenu.Show Called right after a Tower is deselected by the player
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Tower.UnHighlight Called right after a Tower is upgraded Called right after a Tower's TowerModel is changed for any reason (creation, upgrading, etc.)
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Tower.UpdatedModel Called at the end of each round when a Tower's data is saved
Use saveData.metaData to save custom information
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Tower.GetSavedData Called when you load a save file and a Tower's save data get loaded for the tower
Use saveData.metaData to load custom information
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Tower.SetSavedData Called right after Cash is added in a game Tower might be null
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Simulation.AddCash Called right after Cash is removed in a game
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Simulation.RemoveCash Called right after a round starts
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Simulation.OnRoundStart Called right after a round starts
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Simulation.OnRoundEnd Called right after a match ends in defeat
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Simulation.OnDefeat Called before the game shows a hint for a specific round Return 'false' to prevent the original method from running
Equivalent to a HarmonyPrefix on InGame.GetRoundHint the current round the text the hint will have, passed as a ref to allow changes Called after a Removeable is destroyed
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on Map.DestroyRemoveable Called when a ability is cast
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Ability.Activate Called right after an Attack is created
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Attack.Initialise Called right after a Tower's Attack is changed for any reason (creation, upgrading, etc.)
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Attack.UpdatedModel Called right after a Weapon is created
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Weapon.Initialise Called when a weapon fires
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Weapon.SpawnDart Called right after a Tower's WeaponModel is changed for any reason (creation, upgrading, etc.)
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Weapon.UpdatedModel Called right after a Projectile is created
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Projectile.Initialise Called right after a Tower's TowerModel is changed for any reason (creation, upgrading, etc.)
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostFix on Projectile.UpdatedModel Called when all of the existing GameObjects within a match are destroyed Called after a TowerPurchaseButton is created
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on ShopMenu.CreateTowerButton Called before the TowerInventory is initialized
Equivalent to a HarmonyPrefix on TowerInventory.Init Called after a TowerInventory is initialized
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on TowerInventory.Init Called after a banana is picked up
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on Cash.Pickup Called after any button is clicked
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on Button.OnPointerClick Called after the mouse goes over a selectable ui element
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on Button.OnPointerEnter Called after the mouse leaves a button
Equivalent to a HarmonyPostfix on Button.OnPointerExit Extensions for AbilityModels Extensions for AddBehaviorToBloonModels Behavior extensions for attacks Check if this has a specific Behavior The the first Behavior of type T The Behavior you want to remove Remove all Behaviors of type T The Behavior you want to remove Extensions for BloonsTD6Mods (for some reason lol) Get the name of this mod from it's dll name Get the personal mod directory for this specific mod. Useful for keeping this mod's files seperate from other mods. Example: "BloonsTD6/Mods/BloonsTD6 Mod Helper/settings.txt" Get the personal mod directory for this specific mod. Useful for keeping this mod's files seperate from other mods. Example: "BloonsTD6/Mods/BloonsTD6 Mod Helper/settings.txt" Create the mod's directory if it doesn't exist yet? Gets the directory where this mod's settings are or will be stored. Example: "BloonsTD6/Mods/BloonsTD6 Mod Helper/settings.txt" Gets the directory where this mod's settings are or will be stored. Example: "BloonsTD6/Mods/BloonsTD6 Mod Helper/settings.txt" Create the mod's directory if it doesn't exist yet? Extensions for arrays Version of TryCast without the generic restriction Checks if the parameter array has the given types Checks if the parameter array has the given types Checks if the parameter array has the given types Checks if the parameter array has the given types Checks if the parameter array has the given types Checks if the parameter array has the given types Performs the specified action on each element Action to preform on each element Retrieves all the elements that match the conditions defined by the specified predicate. The Predicate delegate that defines the conditions of the elements to search for. Return whether or not there are any elements in this Return whether or not there are any elements in this that match the predicate Return as Il2CppSystem.List Return as Il2CppReferenceArray Return as LockList Return a duplicate of this Array Return a duplicate of this array as type TCast The original Array Type The Cast type Return this with an Item added to it The Type of the Item you want to add Item to add to this Return this with Items added to it The Type of the Items you want to add Items you want to add Return this with Items added to it The Type of the Items you want to add Items you want to add Check if this has any items of type TCast The Type you're checking for Return the first Item of type TCast The Type of the Item you want Return all Items of type TCast The Type of the Items you want Return this with the first Item of type TCast removed The Type of the Item you want to remove Return this with the first Item of type TCast removed The Type of the Item you want to remove The specific Item to remove Return this with all Items of type TCast removed The Type of the Items that you want to remove A string with all array elements printed together with no spaces
Useful for the suffix for Tower IDS like DartMonkey-230 The array Returns the index of the highest tier, then the middle, then the lowest
Breaks ties by Middle Path >> Top Path >> Bottom Path Returns whether an int array is a valid set of tiers for a Tower Extensions for the normal System IEnumerable class Return as Il2CppSystem.List Return as Il2CppReferenceArray Return as LockList Casts a reference array to an IEnumerable of a different Il2cpptype.
Objects that aren't of the specified type will end up as null in the result Returns the argument that maximizes the given value Deconstruct IGrouping to list Repeats each element in the sequence n times, keeping the same order of elements Filters the elements of the IEnumerable by if their Il2Cpp type is T The Il2Cpp type to filter by Extensions for il2cpp ienumerables Get the IEnumerator as type Il2CppSystem.Collections.IEnumerator. with all Items of type TCast removed The Type of the Items that you want to remove Extensions for sending and receiving data in coop Returns true if the player is a host in a co-op game. Works for both lobby and in-game. Send a Message to all players in the lobby Message to send Convert an object to json and send it players or a player in the lobby Object you want to send. The properties of the object will be serialised as JSON. The id of the peer you want the message to go to. Leave null if you want to send to all players Coop code used to distinguish this message from others. Like a lock and key for reading messages Send a string to players or a player in the lobby string message to send. Can be JSON The id of the peer you want the message to go to. Leave null if you want to send to all players Coop code used to distinguish this message from others. Like a lock and key for reading messages Convert messageBytes to an object of type T Type to convert bytes to messageBytes Convert a Message's bytes to an object of type T Type to convert bytes to Message you want to read Convert an object to json and send it players or a player in the lobby Object you want to send. The properties of the object will be serialised as JSON. The id of the peer you want the message to go to. Leave null if you want to send to all players Coop code used to distinguish this message from others. Like a lock and key for reading messages Adding more deconstruct methods to things Deconstruct a rectangle For some reason the normal deconstruct isn't accessible in all places? For some reason the normal deconstruct isn't accessible in all places? Extension for dumping textures from UnityDisplayNodes Only runs when tower placed. Dumps any textures inside of a display node into Ninja Kiwi directory Extensions for Functions Convert a function to an action Convert a function to an action Extensions for Game Returns the instance of the Map Loader. Returns a new SpriteReference that uses the given guid Returns whether or not the player's account is currently flagged/hackerpooled Get the instance of LocalizationManager Schedule a task to execute right now as a Coroutine Schedule a task to execute later on as a Coroutine. By default will wait until the end of this current frame The action you want to execute once it's time to run your task Wait for this to be true before executing task Schedule a task to execute later on as a Coroutine The action you want to execute once it's time to run your task How you want to wait for your task The amount you want to wait /// Wait for this to be true before executing task Get Player LinkAccount. Contains limited info about player's NinjaKiwi account Get the ProfileModel for the Player Get the PlayerService for the player Get GameModel. Same as using Game.instance.model Uses custom message popup to show a message in game. Currently only works in active game sessions and not on Main Menu the Game instance Message body Message title. Will be mod name by default Uses custom message popup to show a message in game. Currently only works in active game sessions and not on Main Menu the Game instance Message body Time to show message on screen Message title. Will be mod name by default Returns the current lobby screen. Returns the current lobby connection. Returns the directory where the Player's Profile.save file is located. Not set until after reaching the Main Menu for the first time Makes a save of Player.Save at the specified path Path to save to Checks if Player is in a game mode that would get them flagged if using mods Returns if Player is in a race Returns if Player is in a public co-op match Returns if Player is in a Odyssey game Get nkGI for the current session. Will be null if not in multiplayer game or lobby Get the Unity Display Factory that manages on screen sprites. This Factory is different from other Factories in the game Get the instance of PopupScreen Get the instance of ShopMenu Get the instance of CommonForegroundScreen Get the instance of CommonBackgroundScreen Get the instance of MenuManager Get the instance of UI Not tested Get the Btd6Player data for the player. Contains different info than PlayerProfile Get all TowerDetailModels Get all HeroDetailModels Get all PowerDetailModels Get player's current Monkey Money amount Add Monkey Money to player's total Monkey Money the Game instance Amount to add Set player's Monkey Money amount the Game instance Value to set Monkey Money to Extensions for Harmony stuff Add a prefix patch Add a postfix patch Extensions for Il2cpp delegates Create a new and seperate copy of this object. Same as using: .Clone().Cast(); Type of object you want to cast to when duplicating. Done automatically Extensions for il2cpp dictionaries Get all of the values from this Dictionary as a list Deconstruct method of IL2CPP KeyValuePairs Extensions for Il2cpp objects Is this Reference equal to another Object's Reference? Object to compare to Check if object is the same type as T Type to check Check if object is the same type as T The casted object if this is of type T Type to check Gets the exact il2cpp type name of an object Box a float into an Il2cpp object Box a int into an Il2cpp object Box a bool into an Il2cpp object Extensions for the InGame class Returns whether or not the player is currently in a game. Get the current Map Get the current Simulation for this InGame session The Game.model that is being used for this InGame.instance Get the main Factory that creates and manages all other Factories Get the Factory for a specific Type. Ex: Getting the Factory that makes Towers Get every Tower that has been created through the Tower Factory Optionally only get Towers whose TowerModel name is this parameter Get all TowerToSimulations /// Optionally only get Towers whose TowerModel name is this parameter Get's all Bloons on the map Get's all existing BloonToSimulations Get's all existing Projectiles on the map Get the current TowerManager for this game session Get's all AbilityToSimulations currently in the game Get's the UnityToSimulation for this game Gets all objects of type T. Does this by returning all objects created by the Factory of type T The type of items you want Sells multiple towers Sells a tower Returns true if the initial co-op handshake has finished and user has co-op game details. The game. Custom API method that changes the game's round set to a custom RoundSetModel. New Round Set Model to use Get the game object that owns all InGame UI elements Get the Cash Manager for the current game InGame instance Index of the cash manager. Default is 0 Get the Player's current cash Add cash to the Player's wallet InGame instance Amount of cash to add to player wallet Set the Player's cash to a specific amount InGame instance Value to set cash to Get the Player's current health Add health to the players current health InGame instance Amount of health to add Set player's health to specific amount InGame instance Value to set health to Get the player's max health Add to the player's max health InGame instance Amount to add to the player's max health Set the player's maximum health to a new value InGame instance Value to set max health to Get collection of popped bloons in this game. Right now only works for current games. Does not store results from loaded games Get the current instance of TowerInventory being used in this game session Set the current round Spawn bloons in game Spawn bloons in game Spawn bloons in game Spawn bloons in game Spawn bloons in game Extensions for Il2cpp Ienumerables Performs the specified action on each element Action to preform on each element Return whether or not there are any Add a weapon to this Attack Model this Weapon to add Remove a weapon from this Attack Model this Weapon to remove Sets the weapon at the given index (default 0) of this attack model to be the given one. this Weapon to add Index within weapons array thrown when index < 0 Recursively get all ProjectileModels for this attack model and all of it's weapons Applies the given ModDisplay to the index'th (or first) DisplayModel in the behaviors of the AttackModel.
If there are no DisplayModels, then this does nothing Adds a new filter to this projectile model Removes a specific filter from this attack model Removes the first filter of a given type from this attack model Extensions for BloonModels (Cross-Game compatable) Return how much cash this bloon would give if popped by number of layers How many layers of bloons to pop, ignoring layer health. If less than 0, calculates for the entire bloon Return the number position of this bloon from the list of all bloons (Game.instance.model.bloons) Spawn this BloonModel on the map right now Create a BloonEmissionModel from this BloonModel Number of bloons in this emission model Space between each bloon in this emission model Return all BloonToSimulations with this BloonModel Return all BloonToSimulations with this BloonModel Set the Display GUID for this BloonModel. Returns the Display GUID for this BloonModel. Return the Base ID of this BloonModel Returns whether or not this BloonModel is a Camo bloon. Set whether or not this BloonModel is a Camo bloon. Returns whether or not this BloonModel is a Regrow bloon. Set whether or not this BloonModel is a Regrow bloon. Adds the Regrow behavior to this BloonModel and sets what Bloon it Regrows into. The ID of the BloonModel that this should regrow into. The rate at which this regrows. Removes the Regrow behavior from this BloonModel. Returns whether or not this BloonModel is a Fortified bloon. Set whether or not this BloonModel is a Fortified bloon. Returns whether or not this BloonModel is an MOAB-Class bloon. Set whether or not this BloonModel is a Fortified bloon. Applies a given ModDisplay to this TowerModel The type of ModDisplay Adds a child to be spawned from the Bloon Gets the SpawnChildrenModel for the bloon, and optionally creates one if it doesn't exist Adds a child to be spawned from the Bloon Removes all spawned children from this BloonModel Removes up to amount of the given Bloon from the spawned children Replaces all spawned child Bloons with the given id with the given ModBloon Replaces all spawned child Bloons that have id oldId with the given ModBloon Replaces all spawned child Bloons of the first ModBloon type with the second ModBloon type Finds the id for a bloon that has the properties of this bloonModel, or null if there isn't one Makes all children of this Bloon Camo, if they can have it Makes all children of this Bloon Regrow, if they can have it Makes all children of this Bloon Fortified, if they can have it Gets the ModBloon associated with this BloonModel
If there is no associated ModBloon, returns null Adds a tag to the BloonModel, if it doesn't already exist Removes a tag from the BloonModel, if it exists Gets the BloonModel for this group Extensions for DisplayModel Applies a given ModDisplay to this DisplayModel The type of ModDisplay Extensions for the GameModel Returns whether or not any TowerModels in have in it's name Returns whether or not any TowerDetailsModels in have in it's name Add a TowerModel to the game.
Using this method is preferable than modifying the GameModel's towers list manually, as this does more things to more fully integrate the tower within the game the GameModel instance TowerModel to add Optionally add a TowerDetailsModel for your towerModel Add multiple TowerModels to the game more efficiently than calling the single method repeatedly. Adds a tower Adds a TowerDetailsModel to the GameModel's TowerSet, taking into account what set of towers it's a part of
For example, a new custom Primary tower would be added right at the end of the primary towers, and right before the start of the military towers The GameModel The TowerDetailsModel to be added The TowerSet of the tower to be added Adds a TowerDetailsModel to the GameModel's TowerSet at a particular index Adds a HeroDetailsModel to the GameModel's HeroSet at a particular index Return all TowerDetailModels Return all ShopTowerDetailModels Return all TowerModels with a specific base id the GameModel instance The base id all towers should share. Example: "DartMonkey" Get a TowerModel from model.towers. NOTE: model.GetTower cannot get custom towers so use this method instead Create a BloonEmissionModel from a bloonModel the GameModel instance The bloon model that these bloons should be Number of Bloons in this emission Space between each bloon in this emission Create a BloonEmissionModel from a bloon's name the GameModel instance Name of bloon. Example: "Red" Number of Bloons in this emission Space between each bloon in this emission Create a single BloonEmission the GameModel instance Name of this bloon. Example: "Red" Time the bloon should be spawned Create a single BloonEmission the GameModel instance Name of this bloon. Example: "Red" Time the bloon should be spawned Creates a BloonGroup Return all AttackModels from every TowerModel in the game Return all WeaponModels from every AttackModel in the game Return all ProjectileModels from every TowerModel in the game Return all AbilityModels from every TowerModel in the game Return all TowerModels that have at least one AbilityModel Fully adds an UpgradeModel to the GameModel Fully adds multiple UpgradeModels to the GameModel Fully adds multiple UpgradeModels to the GameModel Returns the first TowerDetailsModel in that has a towerId of The you are searching for The first TowerDetailsModel found, otherwise returns null Returns whether or not a bloon exists with this name Extension methods for . Sets which bloon this should regrow into. The ID of the bloon this should regrow into Sets which bloon this should regrow into. The ID of the bloon this should regrow into The rate at which this regrows. Returns the ID of the BloonModel that this regrows into. Other miscellaneous extensions for various Model classes Applies the given ModDisplay to this effect Applies the given ModDisplay to this asset path Gets the damageMultiplier field for a DamageModifierModel Updates the to be consistent with the and Extensions for Models Create a new and separate copy of this object. Same as using: .Clone().Cast(); Type of object you want to cast to when duplicating. Done automatically Turns any descendants with "_" as their name into unique named models Finds the first descendent of a given type whose name contains the specified string, or null if not found Finds the first descendent of a given type that matches the given condition, or null if not found Finds the descendents of a given type whose name contains the specified string Finds the descendents of a given type that matches the given condition Finds the descendents of a given type Sets the name of this model, properly appending the type prefix to it.
Also sets the id if it's a ProjectileModel The model itself Duplicates a model and also sets its name to something new Type of object you want to cast to when duplicating. Done automatically Remove multiple Child Dependants at the same time. Equivalent to calling the (weirdly non plural named) Model.RemoveChild method but without having to do an ICollection cast Add multiple Child Dependants at the same time. Equivalent to calling the (weirdly non plural named) Model.AddChild method but without having to do an ICollection cast Returns whether a model has a descendant of a given type Returns whether a model has a descendant of a given type, providing it via the out parameter if so Returns whether a model has a descendant of a given type with a filtered name, providing it via the out parameter if so Extensions for the ModModel (GameMode) class Check if this has a specific Mutator The Mutator you're checking for Check if this has a specific Mutator and return it The Mutator you're checking for Return the first Mutator of type T, or null if there isn't one The Mutator you want Return the index'th Mutator of type T, or null The Mutator you want Return the first Mutator of type T whose name contains the given string, or null The Mutator you want Return all Mutators of type T The Mutator you want Add a Mutator to this ModModel Remove the first Mutator of Type T The Mutator you want to remove Remove the index'th Mutator of Type T The Mutator you want to remove Remove the first Mutator of Type T whose name contains a certain string The Mutator you want to remove Removes a specific mutator from a tower Remove all Mutators of type T Removes all mutators that match a given condition Makes this GameMode use the given RoundSet Makes this GameMode use the given RoundSet Sets the cash this mode starts you with using a If not 0, the new base cash amount to set the starting amount to How much cash to add to the default base cash If not 0, an overall multiplier to the amount of starting cash Sets the life total this mode starts you with using a Sets the maximum life total this mode starts you with using a Sets the round this mode starts at using a Sets the round this mode ends at using a Sets whether Monkey Knowledge is enabled for a gamemode Sets whether Powers are enabled for a game mode Sets whether Continues are enabled for a game mode Sets whether selling towers is enabled for a game mode Sets whether extra income is enabled for a game mode Modifies the Health that Bloons with a given tag have, like for all Moabs The multiplier to apply to Bloons' health The Bloon tag to apply to Prevents a particular TowerSet from being used in this mode The tower set to lock Whether to lock or unlock the tower set Sets the max health and shield amount to 1 Sets whether the Bloons go in reverse Applies a multiplier to all cash gains in the mode (but not starting cash) Sets the portion of cash that should be gotten back when selling (0.7 by default) Extensions for ProfileModels Add a tower to the list of UnlockedTowers The ID of the tower you want to unlock Add a tower to the list of UnlockedTowers only if the TowerModel is in Game.instance.model.towers The ID of the tower you want to unlock If set to true the TowerModel will only be unlocked if it has been registered in the game Returns whether or not the tower was unlocked Extensions for ProjectileModels Get the DamageModel behavior from the list of behaviors Get all Projectile Simulations that have this ProjectileModel Applies a given ModDisplay to this ProjectileModel The type of ModDisplay Returns whether a projectile is able to hit Camo bloons Makes a projectile model able to see Camo or not ] Adds a new filter to this projectile model Removes a specific filter from this projectile model Removes the first filter of the given type from this projectile model Sets the display for this projectile Sets the display for this projectile Extensions for RoundModels Adds a new group of Bloons to this round The round model The id of the Bloon How many Bloons will be emitted When this group starts emitting, in frames (seconds / 60) When this group stops emitting, in frames (seconds / 60) Adds a new group of Bloons to this round The round model How many Bloons will be emitted When this group starts emitting, in frames (seconds / 60) When this group stops emitting, in frames (seconds / 60) Removes all Bloon Groups from the Round Removes all Bloon Groups where the id is as specified Removes the index'th Bloon Group where the id is as specified Replaces BloonGroups of a certain bloonId with ones for a new Id Replaces BloonGroups of a certain bloonId with ones for a new Id Extension methods for support models Makes a support model use a particular ModBuffIcon as its display The support model to apply to The ModBuffIcon type Extensions for TowerDetailsModels Gets the index of this TowerDetailsModel within the GameModel Returns if this TowerDetailModel is actually for a Hero Get the TowerPurchaseButton that is used to buy this specific TowerDetailModel Get the ShopTowerDetails for this TowerDetailModel Makes a copy of this TowerDetailsModel with a new name Makes a copy of this TowerDetailsModel with a new name and index Sets the name of this TowerDetailsModel, following the naming convention of ofther TowerDetailModels. Example, using "NewMonkey" will set the name to "TowerDetailsModel_NewMonkey" Gets the TowerModel for this TowerDetailsModel Extensions for TowerModels Get the name of the BaseTower. Will be different from this TowerModel's name if this TowerModel isn't a BaseTower Has player already unlocked this TowerModel Return all TowerToSimulations with this TowerModel Return all AbilityModel behaviors from this tower, if it has any Return the first ability on the tower Return a specific Ability of the tower. the TowerModel Index of the ability you want. Return all AttackModel behaviors for this TowerModel Return the first AttackModel from this TowerModel, if it has one Return the first AttackModel whose name contains the given string Return one of the AttackModels from this TowerModel. By default will give the first AttackModel The TowerModel Index of the AttackModel you want Recursively get every WeaponModels this TowerModel has Return one of the WeaponModels this TowerModel has. By default will return the first one The TowerModel Index of WeaponModel that you want Return the first WeaponModel this TowerModel has, if it has one. Sell every tower that uses this TowerModel Get the TowerId of this TowerModel. Equivalent to towerModel.name Duplicate this TowerModel with a unique name. Very useful for making custom TowerModels Set's the new towerId of this copy. By default the baseId will be set to this as well Applies a given ModDisplay to this TowerModel The type of ModDisplay Gets the ModTower associated with this TowerModel
If there is no associated ModTower, returns null Gets the specific ModTower associated with this TowerModel
If there is no associated ModTower, returns null Increase the range of a tower and all its attacks by the given amount Not Tested. Use to set the maximum allowed number of this tower Return all TowerDetailModels that share a base id with this towerModel Return the TowerPurchaseButton for this TowerModel. Return the number position of this TowerModel in the list of all tower models Return the current upgrade level of a specific path the TowerModel What tier of upgrade is currently applied to tower If this TowerModel is for a Hero, is this Hero unlocked? Has a specific upgrade for this TowerModel been unlocked already? the TowerModel Upgrade path Tier of upgrade Check if a specific upgrade path is being used/ has any upgrades applied to it the TowerModel Upgrade path to check Check if an upgrade has been applied Check if an upgrade has been applied Return all UpgradeModels that are currently applied to this TowerModel Return the UpgradeModel for a specific upgrade path/tier If this TowerModel is a Hero, return the HeroModel behavior Duplicate this TowerModel with a unique name. Very useful for making custom TowerModels Set's the new towerId of this copy. By default the baseId will be set to this as well Specify a new baseId. Set this if you want a baseId other than the newTowerId Check if this tower has speficif upgrade tiers Sets a TowerModel's tiers Format's the tower's name with its tiers Format's the tower's name with its tiers Gets the tower set (vanilla or modded) of this tower in the form of a string Gets whether a Tower/Hero is a base one added by the vanilla game. Extensions for UpgradeModels Return whether or not this upgrade has been unlocked by the player Gets the ModUpgrade associated with this UpgradeModel
If there is no associated ModUpgrade, returns null Gets the UpgradeModel that this UpgradePathModel uses Gets the ModUpgrade that this UpgradePathModel is for, or null if it's vanilla Extensions for WeaponModels Sets the emission for a WeaponModel, properly handling the child dependents Sets the emission for a WeaponModel, properly handling the child dependents Sets ejectX/ejectY/ejectZ all at once Sets ejectX/ejectY/ejectZ all at once Gets the eject position of the weapon as a vector Extensions for NK_TextMeshProUGuis Gets the localized text for the component Changes the text in the localization manager for this component Extensions for Bloons Return the DisplayNode for this bloon Return the UnityDisplayNode for this bloon. Is apart of DisplayNode. Needed to modify sprites Creates a new BloonToSimulation based off of this Bloon and stores it for possible later use. It will automatically destroyed when this Bloon is destroyed Return the Id of this Bloon Return the Factory that creates Bloons Return the existing BloonToSimulation for this specific Bloon. Tests whether a project will pop this bloon Returns whether or not the bloon was popped rather than leaked. True if it was popped, false if it was leaked or not destroyed yet Set bloon to be camo or not. Will change bloonModel to camo version if it exists the Bloon Should bloon be camo Set bloon to be fortified or not. Will change bloonModel to fortified version if it exists the Bloon Should bloon be fortified Set bloon to be regrow or not. Will change bloonModel to regrow version if it exists the Bloon Should bloon be regrow Remove current statuses from bloon the Bloon Should remove camo if present? Should remove fortify if present? Should remove regrow if present? Set the statuses of the bloon. Will change bloonModel if one exists with these statuses the Bloon Should have camo? Should have fortify? Should have regrow?
